The employed are defined as all persons aged 15-89 who during the reference week: - performed for at least one hour any work generating pay or income, i.e. were employed by a public company/institution or private employer,, worked on their own (or leased) agricultural farm, or conducted their own economic activity outside agriculture, assisted (without pay) in work on family agricultural farm or in conducting family economic activity outside agriculture, - had work but did not perform it due to:  • sickness, or rest leave, leave related to parenthood (maternity, parental, paternity or childcare), organization of working time (work system or overtime receipt), job-related training, • the seasonal nature of the work, if they continued to regularly fulfill work or business-related tasks and duties (excluding legal or administrative duties) during the off-season, • other reasons, if the expected period of absence from work does not exceed 3 months. - did not exceed 3 months.  According to international standards, among employees are also included persons performing outwork and apprentices with whom enterprises or natural persons signed a contract for occupational training or learning skills for a particular job (if they receive a payment). The employed do not include: volunteers and unpaid interns, people working in individual agriculture engaged in the production of agricultural products exclusively or primarily for their own consumption.

metadata.name: 8.C.2 Percentage of employed persons in relation to the number of persons employed in the national economy
metadata.goal: Goal 8. Decent work and economic growth
metadata.priority: Improving the quality of human capital for a modern economy, increasing the employment rate, including promoting stable forms of employment
metadata.definition: Share of paid employees in total number of employed persons in national economy.
metadata.methodology: Data regarding employment concern persons performing work providing earnings or income.  Employed persons include:  a. persons employed on the basis of an employment relationship (i.e. employment contract, appointment, election or nomination) or service relationship for a definite and indefinite period of time, according to full-time and part-time work schedules; b. employers and own-account workers including contributing family workers; c. outworkers; d. agents (until 2021); e. members of agricultural production cooperatives; f. clergy fulfilling priestly obligations.  When determining the number of employees, each working person is listed only once. For this purpose, a main workplace is assigned to her. Data on people working in the national economy are presented in relation to their main workplace. Data on the number of people employed in the national economy in the main workplace are given in persons as at the end of December.

metadata.name: 8.C.3 Percentage of young people neither in employment nor in education and training (NEET)
metadata.goal: Goal 8. Decent work and economic growth
metadata.priority: Improving the quality of human capital for a modern economy, increasing the employment rate, including promoting stable forms of employment
metadata.definition: Share of unemployed or inactive 15-24 year olds who do not continue their education in the formal education system and do not further their education/training in the non-formal education system (within 4 weeks before the survey) in the total population in the same age group. Indicator's numerator refers to those who meet two conditions:  - are not employed (i.e. are unemployed or economically inactive), - did not participate in any form of education during four weeks preceding the survey.  Indicator's denominator is a population in a given age group, which the indicator concerns, excluding persons who did not answer the question concerning 'participation in regular education and training'.
metadata.methodology: The data comes from the representative Labour Force Survey (LFS), conducted on a quarterly basis using the continuous observation method (movable survey week), allowing to illustrate the labor market situation over the entire quarter. The survey covers persons aged 15-89 who are members of households in drawn dwellings. Outside the scope of the survey are members of households residing (the total duration of actual and planned stay is taken into account) 12 months or more abroad and in collective households (such as staff hotels, dormitories, dormitories, nursing homes, etc.) and homeless persons. The basic criterion for dividing the population from the point of view of labor force participation is work, i.e. the fact of doing, having or seeking work. According to international standards, the general population can be divided into three basic categories: employed, unemployed and economically inactive. The employed and unemployed constitute the economically active population.

metadata.name: 8.D.1 Number of women who did not start work after childbirth per 10 thous. inhabitants
metadata.goal: Goal 8. Decent work and economic growth
metadata.priority: Professional activation, taking into account particularly socially vulnerable groups and shaping a friendly work environment
metadata.definition: The number of women, who after giving a birth, registered in the respective labour office to find a job per 10 thousand of population.
metadata.methodology: The definition of registered unemployed persons is specified by the Law of 20 April 2004 on promoting employment and labour market institutions (Journal of Laws 2020 item 1409, with later amendments).An unemployed person is a person who is not employed or performing any other kind of paid work, available for full-time work (or in case of disabled persons — available for at least half of the full work-time required for a given occupation or service), are not attending any full-time school with the exception of schools for adults or taking an extra-curriculum exam in such schools or tertiary schools in the evening or weekend education system, and are registered in the powiat labour office respective to the person’s permanent or temporary place of residence, seeking employment or any other paid work if they, among others:1) is at least 18 years old and2) is less than 60 in case of women and less than 65 in case of men;3) did not become entitled to benefits from retirement pay or disability pension, family pension, education pension, social pension, does not receive pre-retirement benefit and allowance, rehabilitation benefit, sick benefit, maternity benefit or benefit of an amount equal to maternity benefit;4) is not an owner or a holder (independent or dependent) of an agricultural real estate with an area of agricultural land exceeding 2 convertible ha;5) is not eligible for retirement and disability insurance from constant work as a spouse or a household member on a farm with an area of agricultural land exceeding 2 convertible ha;6) does not have an entry in the register of economic activity or is not eligible –at the basis of separate legislation – for social insurance with an exception of farmers social insurance;7) is not a pre-trial detainee or a convicts serving punishment in prison;8) does not receive monthly income exceeding half of the monthly minimum wages and salaries for work excluding incomes received from interests or other incomes from cash kept on a bank accounts;9) does not receive permanent benefit on the basis of provisions of the regulations on social assistance;10) does not receive nursing benefit on the basis of provisions of the regulations on family allowances;11) does not receive training allowances after termination of employment.

metadata.name: 8.D.2 Long-term unemployment as a percentage of the total unemployment (aged 20-64) (LFS)
metadata.goal: Goal 8. Decent work and economic growth
metadata.priority: Professional activation, taking into account particularly socially vulnerable groups and shaping a friendly work environment
metadata.definition: Percentage of registered unemployed persons who have been unemployed for more than 1 year since registering with the employment office in the total number of registered unemployed persons.
metadata.methodology: Registered unemployed persons are persons who are aged 18 or more and have not reached the retirement age, are not employed or performing any other kind of paid work, available for full- time work and are registered in the powiat labour office respective to the person's permanent or temporary place of residence, seeking employment or any other paid work.Among the unemployed persons are included disabled persons—are able and ready to take employment at least half the full work-time required within a given occupation or service, are not attending any full-time school with the exception of schools for adults (or taking extra curriculum exam in such schools) or tertiary schools in the evening or weekend education system or seeking employment or any other paid work, with additional provisions concerning the sources of income, included in the mentioned law.Detailed criteria defining a registered unemployed person are set out in the Act of 20 April 2004 on the promotion of employment and labour market institutions (Journal of Laws No. Journal of Laws 2024, item 475, as amended).Data as of 31 December.
metadata.source: Ministry of Family, Labour and Social Policy

metadata.name: 8.D.3 Unemployment rate (LFS)
metadata.goal: Goal 8. Decent work and economic growth
metadata.priority: Professional activation, taking into account particularly socially vulnerable groups and shaping a friendly work environment
metadata.definition: Share of the unemployed by LFS (of a given category) in the economically active population by LFS (of a given category). In the case of the Labour Force Survey, the definition applies to unemployed (aged 15-74 years) and economically active persons (aged 15-89 years), being the sum of the employed and the unemployed.
metadata.methodology: The Population Economic Activity Survey (LFS) is one of the basic studies conducted by the Central Statistical Office in the area of the labour market. This study enables the current assessment of the degree of utilization of labour resources, and at the same time allows for a broader characterization of individual population groups distinguished by their status on the labour market (working, unemployed) or remaining outside the labour market (inactive). The LFS is the Polish edition of the European Labour Force Survey (ELS). Labour Force Survey/LFS) and has been conducted in Poland on a quarterly basis since May 1992. This is a continuous survey carried out throughout the year on a sample of the population staying or intending to stay in the country for at least 12 months, living in households in randomly selected apartments.The LFS unemployment rate represents the share of unemployed people in the number of active people.The unemployed according to the LFS are people aged 15-74 who meet three conditions at the same time:- during the week in question were not working persons,- were actively looking for a job, i.e. took specific actions within 4 weeks (including the last week tested) to find a job,- were ready (able) to work in the two weeks following the week of study.
metadata.notes: Since 2023, data on the resident population of Poland from balance sheets compiled on the basis of the results of the 2021 National Population and Housing Census have been used to generalise the results of the Population Activity Survey (LFS) to the general population. The presented data for the years 2021-2022 have been recalculated in accordance with the new basis for generalization of results, therefore they are not fully comparable with earlier periods.

metadata.name: 8.D.4 Employment rate of persons in working age
metadata.goal: Goal 8. Decent work and economic growth
metadata.priority: Professional activation, taking into account particularly socially vulnerable groups and shaping a friendly work environment
metadata.definition: Share of employed persons in population. The indicator may relate to the employed in terms of total or given group.
metadata.methodology: Data comes from a sample survey: Labour Force Survey (LFS). The survey is conducted on a quarterly basis, using continuous observation (movable survey week) what allows presenting the situation on the labour market during a whole quarter. The survey covers persons aged 15-89 who are members of households in dwellings selected on a random basis. Outside the scope of the survey are members of households staying (the total duration of actual and planned stay is taken into account) 12 months or more abroad and in collective households (such as staff hotels, dormitories, boarding schools, nursing homes, etc.) and homeless people. In the context of economic activity - work is the main criterion in dividing the population, i.e. performing, holding or seeking work. According to the international standards, the tree main categories are distinguished: employed, unemployed and economically inactive persons.The employed and unemployed are the economically active population. The employed are defined as all persons aged 15-89 who during the reference week: - performed for at least one hour any work generating pay or income, i.e. were employed by a public company/institution or private employer,, worked on their own (or leased) agricultural farm, or conducted their own economic activity outside agriculture, assisted (without pay) in work on family agricultural farm or in conducting family economic activity outside agriculture, - had work but did not perform it due to:  • sickness, or rest leave, leave related to parenthood (maternity, parental, paternity or childcare), organization of working time (work system or overtime receipt), job-related training, • the seasonal nature of the work, if they continued to regularly fulfill work or business-related tasks and duties (excluding legal or administrative duties) during the off-season, • other reasons, if the expected period of absence from work does not exceed 3 months. - did not exceed 3 months.  According to international standards, among employees are also included persons performing outwork and apprentices with whom enterprises or natural persons signed a contract for occupational training or learning skills for a particular job (if they receive a payment). The employed do not include: volunteers and unpaid interns, people working in individual agriculture engaged in the production of agricultural products exclusively or primarily for their own consumption.

metadata.name: 8.D.5 Activity rate of persons aged 50-89
metadata.goal: Goal 8. Decent work and economic growth
metadata.priority: Professional activation, taking into account particularly socially vulnerable groups and shaping a friendly work environment
metadata.definition: The share of economically active persons (total or of given category) in the total of population or population of given category (in %). Average data per year.
metadata.methodology: Data comes from a sample survey: Labour Force Survey (LFS). The survey is conducted on a quarterly basis, using continuous observation (movable survey week) what allows presenting the situation on the labour market during a whole quarter. The survey covers persons aged 15-89 who are members of households in dwellings selected on a random basis. Outside the scope of the survey are members of households staying (the total duration of actual and planned stay is taken into account) 12 months or more abroad and in collective households (such as staff hotels, dormitories, boarding schools, nursing homes, etc.) and homeless people. In the context of economic activity - work is the main criterion in dividing the population, i.e. performing, holding or seeking work. According to the international standards, the tree main categories are distinguished: employed, unemployed and economically inactive persons.The employed and unemployed are the economically active population. The employed are defined as all persons aged 15-89 who during the reference week:  - performed for at least one hour any work generating pay or income, i.e. were employed by a public company/institution or private employer,, worked on their own (or leased) agricultural farm, or conducted their own economic activity outside agriculture, assisted (without pay) in work on family agricultural farm or in conducting family economic activity outside agriculture, - had work but did not perform it due to:   • sickness, or rest leave, leave related to parenthood (maternity, parental, paternity or childcare), organization of working time (work system or overtime receipt), job-related training, • the seasonal nature of the work, if they continued to regularly fulfill work or business-related tasks and duties (excluding legal or administrative duties) during the off-season, • other reasons, if the expected period of absence from work does not exceed 3 months - did not exceed 3 months.  According to international standards, among employees are also included persons performing outwork and apprentices with whom enterprises or natural persons signed a contract for occupational training or learning skills for a particular job (if they receive a payment). The employed do not include: volunteers and unpaid interns, people working in individual agriculture engaged in the production of agricultural products exclusively or primarily for their own consumption. Unemployed persons are persons aged 15-74 who simultaneously fulfil three conditions: - within the reference week were not employed, - were actively looking for work, i.e. for over 4 weeks (the reference week being the fourth one) had been involved in concrete actions aimed at finding a job, - were available to take up work within two weeks after the reference week. Persons who were not seeking work because they had already found a job, were only waiting to start work within the period no longer than 3 months, and were available for this job are also included in the category of the unemployed.

metadata.name: 8.D.6 Number of registered unemployed persons with no work seniority per 1 thous. population
metadata.goal: Goal 8. Decent work and economic growth
metadata.priority: Professional activation, taking into account particularly socially vulnerable groups and shaping a friendly work environment
metadata.definition: The number of people who have not worked so far, registered in district labour offices as unemployed per 1000 population aged 18-59/64 years.
metadata.methodology: Registered unemployed persons are persons who are aged 18 or more and have not reached the retirement age, are not employed or performing any other kind of paid work, available for full- time work and are registered in the powiat labour office respective to the person's permanent or temporary place of residence, seeking employment or any other paid work.Among the unemployed persons are included disabled persons—are able and ready to take employment at least half the full work-time required within a given occupation or service, are not attending any full-time school with the exception of schools for adults (or taking extra curriculum exam in such schools) or tertiary schools in the evening or weekend education system or seeking employment or any other paid work, with additional provisions concerning the sources of income, included in the mentioned law.Detailed criteria defining a registered unemployed person are set out in the Act of 20 April 2004 on the promotion of employment and labour market institutions (Journal of Laws No. Journal of Laws 2024, item 475, as amended).The number of unemployed persons registered without work experience was converted into 1000 people of working age, i.e. aged 18-59 years (women) or 18-64 years (men).Data as of 31 December.
metadata.source: Ministry of Family, Labour and Social Policy / Statistics Poland

metadata.name: 8.D.7 Employment rate of disabled persons aged 16-64
metadata.goal: Goal 8. Decent work and economic growth
metadata.priority: Professional activation, taking into account particularly socially vulnerable groups and shaping a friendly work environment
metadata.definition: The share of employed disabled persons of given category in the total of disabled persons of that category (in %).
metadata.methodology: The data comes from the representative Labour Force Survey (LFS), conducted on a quarterly basis using the continuous observation method (moving week of the survey), allowing to illustrate the labour market situation over the entire quarter. The survey covers persons aged 15-89 who are members of households in drawn dwellings. Outside the scope of the survey, there are members of households staying (the total duration of actual and planned stay is taken into account) 12 months or more abroad and in collective households (such as staff hotels, dormitories, boarding schools, nursing homes, etc.) and homeless persons.In the context of economic activity – work is the main criterion in dividing the population, i.e. performing, holding or seeking work. According to the international standards, the tree main categories are distinguished: employed, unemployed and economically inactive persons. The employed, unemployed are the economically active population.Among the employed are included all persons aged 15-89 years who during the reference week:1) performed for at least 1 hour an income-generating or earning job, i.e., were employed by a public company/institution or private employer, worked on their own (or leased) agricultural farm or conducted their own economic activity outside agriculture, assisted (without pay) in running a family farm or family economic activity outside agriculture,2) had work, but did not perform it:- due to to sickness, vacation, parenthood-related leave (maternity leave, paternity or parental leave), working time arrangement (work system or compensation for overtime hours), training related to the performed work,- due to the seasonal nature of work, if they still regularly performed off-season work or business-related tasks and responsibilities (excluding legal or administrative obligations),- for other reasons, if the expected period of absence from work does not exceed 3 months.According to international standards, among employees are also included persons performing outwork and apprentices with whom enterprises or natural persons signed a contract for occupational training or learning skills for a particular job (if they receive a payment).Among the employed are not included: volunteers and unpaid interns, persons employed in private agriculture who produce agricultural exclusively or primarily for their own use.Population of disabled persons was legally separated from the total population aged 16-89, based on the legal criterion. Persons with disabilities include persons who are 16 years of age and in accordance with the provisions of the Act of 27 August 1997 on vocational and social rehabilitation and employment of disabled persons (Journal of Laws of 2016, item 2046, as amended). d.) have a disability degree certificate or an equivalent certificate:- significant degree of disability - this includes persons with the 1st disability group, or a certificate of total inability to work and live independently, or a certificate of permanent or long-term incapacity to work on a farm with the right to a care allowance,- moderate degree of disability - this includes people with disability group II or a certificate of total inability to work,- slight degree of disability - this includes persons with disability group III, or a certificate of partial inability to work and the advisability of retraining, or a certificate of permanent or long-term incapacity to work on a farm without entitlement to care allowance.

metadata.name: 8.D.8 Employment rate of persons aged 15-29
metadata.goal: Goal 8. Decent work and economic growth
metadata.priority: Professional activation, taking into account particularly socially vulnerable groups and shaping a friendly work environment
metadata.definition: Share of employed persons in population. The indicator may relate to the employed in terms of total or given group.
metadata.methodology: Data comes from a sample survey: Labour Force Survey (LFS). The survey is conducted on a quarterly basis, using continuous observation (movable survey week) what allows presenting the situation on the labour market during a whole quarter. The survey covers persons aged 15-89 who are members of households in dwellings selected on a random basis. Outside the scope of the survey are members of households staying (the total duration of actual and planned stay is taken into account) 12 months or more abroad and in collective households (such as staff hotels, dormitories, boarding schools, nursing homes, etc.) and homeless people. In the context of economic activity - work is the main criterion in dividing the population, i.e. performing, holding or seeking work. According to the international standards, the tree main categories are distinguished: employed, unemployed and economically inactive persons.The employed and unemployed are the economically active population. The employed are defined as all persons aged 15-89 who during the reference week: - performed for at least one hour any work generating pay or income, i.e. were employed by a public company/institution or private employer,, worked on their own (or leased) agricultural farm, or conducted their own economic activity outside agriculture, assisted (without pay) in work on family agricultural farm or in conducting family economic activity outside agriculture, - had work but did not perform it due to:  • sickness, or rest leave, leave related to parenthood (maternity, parental, paternity or childcare), organization of working time (work system or overtime receipt), job-related training, • the seasonal nature of the work, if they continued to regularly fulfill work or business-related tasks and duties (excluding legal or administrative duties) during the off-season, • other reasons, if the expected period of absence from work does not exceed 3 months. - did not exceed 3 months.  According to international standards, among employees are also included persons performing outwork and apprentices with whom enterprises or natural persons signed a contract for occupational training or learning skills for a particular job (if they receive a payment). The employed do not include: volunteers and unpaid interns, people working in individual agriculture engaged in the production of agricultural products exclusively or primarily for their own consumption.

metadata.name: 8.D.9 Percentage of adults participating in education or training (aged 25–64)
metadata.goal: Goal 8. Decent work and economic growth
metadata.priority: Professional activation, taking into account particularly socially vulnerable groups and shaping a friendly work environment
metadata.definition: Share of the number of persons aged 25-64 participating in education or training (in the period of four weeks before the survey); in the total number of population in the same age group.
metadata.methodology: Data (annual average) were compiled on the basis of the results of the sample survey: Labour Force Survey (LFS) conducted on a quaterly basis, using the method of continouous observation (movable survey week), allowing to illustrate the situation on the labour market during the entire quarter. The survey covers persons aged 15-89, who are members of households in dwellings selected on a random basis. The scope of the survey does not include household members staying (the total actual and planned stay is taken into account) 12 months or more abroad and in collective households (such as: employee hotels, student dormitories, boarding houses, social care centres etc.) and the homeless. In the context of economic activity - work is the main criterion in dividing the population, i.e. performing, holding or seeking work. According to the international standards, the three main categories are distinguished: employed, unemployed and economically inactive persons. The employed, unemployed are the economically active population. The indicator includes training of adults at schools for adults as well as getting and improving general knowledge, professional skills and competences outside school (in the four weeks preceding the survey) by persons who completed compulsory education. The adult education includes the following forms: • formal education - defined as institutionally organized learning activities through educational programmes and trainings leading to vocational qualifications; • non-formal education - defined as institutionally organized learning activities, however, outside educational programmes and trainings leading to vocational qualifications; • informal education - defined as not institutionally organized intentional or unintentional learning activities.
metadata.availability: The average data per yer; since 2010

metadata.name: 9.A.1 Share of industrial enterprises with expenditures on innovation
metadata.priority: Increased competitiveness of the region by increasing the research and innovation potential (Development of institutional potential for research and development, supporting cooperation between the research sector and the economy)
metadata.definition: Percentage ratio of industrial enterprises incurring expenditure on innovation activities to all industrial enterprises.
metadata.methodology: Innovative industrial enterprises are industrial enterprises that introduced at least one product or business process innovation (new or improved product or new or improved business process) to the market in the period under review - until 2018 - process innovation (new or significantly improved product or new or significantly improved process).Expenditures on innovation activities in enterprises by type of innovation activity are all expenses on product innovations in business processes (until 2015 process (current and investment) innovations), regardless of the source of financing, incurred in the reporting year, for work completed successfully (i.e. implementation of the innovation), unfinished (continued) and interrupted or abandoned.
metadata.availability: Annual data, since 2008; since 2022 data in two-year cycles

metadata.name: 9.A.10 Share of patent applications in voivodship in a total number of patent applications
metadata.priority: Increased competitiveness of the region by increasing the research and innovation potential (Development of institutional potential for research and development, supporting cooperation between the research sector and the economy)
metadata.definition: The indicator determines the share of invention applications from a given voivodeship in the total number of inventions submitted to the Patent Office, expressed in %.
metadata.methodology: A reported invention (Patent application) is a solution to a specific problem of a technical nature, reported for protection to the appropriate office accepting the application, in order to obtain patent protection for a given invention.Source of definition: Act of 30 June 2000 - Industrial Property Law Place of publication: (Journal of Laws of 2023, item 1170).National procedure for reporting inventions for patenting (National procedure) - a procedure for all types of applications submitted directly to the patent office of a given country - from the territory of that country and from abroad under the Paris Convention of 1883.

metadata.name: 9.A.2 Share of expenditures on innovation activity in enterprises in national expenditures
metadata.priority: Increased competitiveness of the region by increasing the research and innovation potential (Development of institutional potential for research and development, supporting cooperation between the research sector and the economy)
metadata.definition: Structure of expenditure on innovation activities by voivodeship.
metadata.methodology: Innovation activity is the entirety of scientific, technical, organizational, financial and commercial activities that lead or are intended to lead to the implementation of innovation. Some of these activities are innovative in nature, while others are not new but are necessary for the implementation of innovation. Innovation activity also includes research and development (R&D) activities that are not directly related to the creation of a specific innovation.Expenditures on innovation activity in enterprises by type of innovation activity are all expenses on product innovations in business processes (up to 2015, process (current and investment)), regardless of the source of financing, incurred in the reporting year, on work completed successfully (i.e. implementation of the innovation), unfinished (continued) and interrupted or abandoned.
metadata.availability: Annual data, since 2008; since 2022 data in two-year cycles

metadata.name: 9.A.3 Share of industrial enterprises that cooperated in terms of innovation activity in total number of enterprises
metadata.priority: Increased competitiveness of the region by increasing the research and innovation potential (Development of institutional potential for research and development, supporting cooperation between the research sector and the economy)
metadata.definition: Percentage ratio of industrial enterprises employing 10 or more persons cooperating in the field of innovation activities to all industrial enterprises.
metadata.methodology: Industrial innovation enterprises are industrial enterprises that introduced at least one product innovation in business processes (new or improved product or new or improved business process) to the market in the period under review - until 2018 - process innovation (new or significantly improved product or new or significantly improved process).Cooperation in the field of innovation activity means active participation in joint projects concerning innovation activity with other enterprises or non-commercial institutions. Such cooperation may be prospective and long-term in nature and does not have to entail direct, measurable economic benefits for the participating partners. Simply ordering work from external contractors, without active participation in their implementation, should not be considered cooperation in the field of innovation activity.
metadata.availability: Annual data, since 2008; since 2022 data in two-year cycles

metadata.name: 9.A.4 Share of enterprises in the service sector that cooperated in terms of innovation activity in total number of enterprises
metadata.priority: Increased competitiveness of the region by increasing the research and innovation potential (Development of institutional potential for research and development, supporting cooperation between the research sector and the economy)
metadata.definition: Percentage ratio of service sector enterprises employing 10 or more people cooperating in the field of innovation activities to all service sector enterprises.
metadata.methodology: Innovative enterprises from the service sector are enterprises that introduced at least one product innovation in business processes (new or improved product or new or improved business process) to the market in the period under review - until 2018 - process innovation (new or significantly improved product or new or significantly improved process).Cooperation in the field of innovation activity means active participation in joint projects concerning innovation activity with other enterprises or non-commercial institutions. Such cooperation may be prospective and long-term in nature and does not have to entail direct, measurable economic benefits for the participating partners. Simply ordering work from external contractors, without active participation in their implementation, should not be considered cooperation in the field of innovation activity.
metadata.availability: Annual data, since 2008; since 2022 data in two-year cycles

metadata.name: 9.A.5 Share of innovative enterprises in total number of enterprises
metadata.priority: Increased competitiveness of the region by increasing the research and innovation potential (Development of institutional potential for research and development, supporting cooperation between the research sector and the economy)
metadata.definition: Share of innovative enterprises in the total number of enterprises expressed in %.
metadata.methodology: Innovative enterprises are enterprises that introduced at least one product or business process innovation (new or improved product or new or improved business process) to the market in the period under review - until 2018 - process innovation (new or significantly improved product or new or significantly improved process).
metadata.availability: Annual data, since 2008; since 2022 data in two-year cycles

metadata.name: 9.A.6 Share of net revenues from sales of innovative products for export in total net revenues from sales in industrial enterprises
metadata.priority: Increased competitiveness of the region by increasing the research and innovation potential (Development of institutional potential for research and development, supporting cooperation between the research sector and the economy)
metadata.definition: The share of the value of net revenues from the sale of innovative products for export in the total value of net revenues from the sale of products of industrial enterprises employing 10 or more people.
metadata.methodology: Net revenues from the sale of products include amounts due - excluding value added tax - from the sale of products (goods and services), goods and materials.Innovative activity involves the engagement of enterprises in various scientific, technical, organizational, financial and commercial activities that lead or are intended to lead to the implementation of innovations. Some of these activities are innovative in nature, while others are not new but are necessary for the implementation of innovations. Innovative activity also includes research and development (R&D) activities that are not directly related to the creation of a specific innovation.

metadata.name: 9.A.7 Intramural expenditure on R&D in relation to GDP
metadata.priority: Increased competitiveness of the region by increasing the research and innovation potential (Development of institutional potential for research and development, supporting cooperation between the research sector and the economy)
metadata.definition: Total internal expenditure incurred in a given year on R&D activities conducted in the reporting unit, regardless of the source of funds, in relation to GDP.
metadata.methodology: Research and Development (R&D) Creative work undertaken in a methodical manner to increase the resources of knowledge - including knowledge of mankind, culture and society - and to create new applications for existing knowledge.R&D includes: 1. scientific research - basic research - applied research (identical to application research) 2. development work.Internal R&D expenditures: Expenditures incurred in the reporting year for R&D work performed in the reporting entity, regardless of the source of the funds. They include both current expenditures and capital expenditures on fixed assets related to R&D activities, but do not include depreciation of fixed assets.

metadata.name: 9.A.8 Intramural expenditure on R&D per 1 inhabitant
metadata.priority: Increased competitiveness of the region by increasing the research and innovation potential (Development of institutional potential for research and development, supporting cooperation between the research sector and the economy)
metadata.definition: Total internal expenditure incurred in a given year for R&D activities conducted in the reporting unit, regardless of the source of funds, per capita.
metadata.methodology: Internal R&D expenditures: Expenditures incurred during the reporting year for R&D work performed within the reporting entity, regardless of the source of the funds. They include both current expenditures and capital expenditures for fixed assets related to R&D activities, but do not include depreciation of fixed assets.

metadata.name: 9.A.9 Internal R&D personnel per 1000 economically active persons
metadata.priority: Increased competitiveness of the region by increasing the research and innovation potential (Development of institutional potential for research and development, supporting cooperation between the research sector and the economy)
metadata.definition: Share of internal R&D personnel per 1,000 economically active persons (economically active according to BAEL, aged 15-89)
metadata.methodology: Internal R&D personnel, Working in R&D English name: Internal R&D personnel Definition: Persons working (employees, working owners and unpaid helping family members) in a statistical unit who contribute to the internal research and development activities of that unit.FTE - full-time equivalents - conversion units used to determine the actual involvement in research and development activities; one full-time equivalent (FTE) means one person-year devoted exclusively to R&D activities.Research and experimental development (R&D) - creative work undertaken in a methodical manner in order to increase the resources of knowledge - including knowledge about mankind, culture and society - and to create new applications for existing knowledge.

metadata.name: 9.B.1 Gross domestic product per capita
metadata.priority: Strong and intelligent economy strengthening the competitiveness of the region (Strengthening the mechanisms of innovation transfer in the economy and development of intelligent specializations)
metadata.definition: The ratio of the value of Gross Domestic Product at current prices to the number of inhabitants of a given area.
metadata.methodology: Gross domestic product (GDP) reflects the final result of the activities of all entities of the national economy (resident units) in a given year. The detailed definition and methodology for calculating GDP are specified in Regulation (EU) No 549/2013 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 21 May 2013 on the European system of national and regional accounts in the European Union (ESA 2010).

metadata.name: 9.B.2 Voivodship's share in generating gross domestic product
metadata.priority: Strong and intelligent economy strengthening the competitiveness of the region (Strengthening the mechanisms of innovation transfer in the economy and development of intelligent specializations)
metadata.definition: The indicator determines the share of a voivodeship in the gross domestic product, expressed in %.
metadata.methodology: Gross domestic product (GDP) illustrates the final result of the activity of all entities of the national economy (resident producer units – domestic) in a given year. The exact definition and methodology for calculating GDP is contained in the regulation (EU) No 549/2013 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 21 May 2013 on the European system of national and regional accounts in the European Union (ESA 2010).

metadata.name: 9.B.3 Sold production of industry per capita
metadata.priority: Strong and intelligent economy strengthening the competitiveness of the region (Strengthening the mechanisms of innovation transfer in the economy and development of intelligent specializations)
metadata.definition: The value of sales of products (goods and services) of industrial enterprises' own production, divided by the number of inhabitants.
metadata.methodology: Sold industrial production is the basic measure of economic activity (i.e. industrial, construction and assembly, transport and other activities) of industrial enterprises and companies, i.e. economic entities classified according to PKD2007 to sections B, C, D, E.Sold industrial production is the value expressed in current basic prices, i.e. excluding the goods and services tax (VAT), excise tax, and including the value of received subsidies for products (goods and services).

metadata.name: 9.B.4 Investment outlays per capita
metadata.priority: Strong and intelligent economy strengthening the competitiveness of the region (Strengthening the mechanisms of innovation transfer in the economy and development of intelligent specializations)
metadata.definition: Investment expenditures incurred per capita.
metadata.methodology: Investment outlays are financial or material outlays aimed at creating new fixed assets or improving (rebuilding, expanding, reconstructing or modernizing) existing fixed assets, as well as outlays on the so-called initial investment equipment. Investment outlays are divided into outlays on fixed assets and other outlays. Outlays on fixed assets are outlays on: - buildings and structures (including buildings and premises as well as civil and water engineering structures) including, among others, construction and assembly works, - design and cost documentation, - machines, technical devices and tools (including instruments, movables and equipment), - means of transport.

metadata.name: 9.B.5 Entities of the national economy entered in the REGON register per 1 thous. population
metadata.priority: Strong and intelligent economy strengthening the competitiveness of the region (Strengthening the mechanisms of innovation transfer in the economy and development of intelligent specializations)
metadata.definition: The number of entities of the national economy (legal persons, organizational units without legal personality and individuals conducting business activities) registered in the REGON register per 1,000 population.
metadata.methodology: Entity of the national economy is a legal person, an organizational unit without legal personality and an individual conducting business activity.Information on entities of the national economy in the REGON register (excluding individuals running only individual farms) presents data on the number and structure of entities of the national economy entered in the REGON register.

metadata.name: 9.B.6 Entities of the national economy entered in the REGON register per 1 thous.rural population
metadata.priority: Strong and intelligent economy strengthening the competitiveness of the region (Strengthening the mechanisms of innovation transfer in the economy and development of intelligent specializations)
metadata.definition: The number of entities of the national economy (legal persons, organizational units without legal personality and a natural person conducting business activity) registered in the REGON register per 1,000 people in rural areas.
metadata.methodology: Information on entities of the national economy in the REGON register (excluding individuals running only individual farms) presents data on the number and structure of entities of the national economy entered in the REGON register.An entity of the national economy is a legal person, an organizational unit without legal personality and an individual conducting business activity.

metadata.name: 9.B.7 Foreign capital per capita of working age
metadata.priority: Strong and intelligent economy strengthening the competitiveness of the region (Strengthening the mechanisms of innovation transfer in the economy and development of intelligent specializations)
metadata.definition: Foreign capital per capita of working age, i.e. for men aged 18-64, for women aged 18-59.
metadata.methodology: Foreign capital is capital contributed to an entity in the form of financial resources (cash, shares, bonds), tangible fixed assets (machinery, equipment, means of transport, real estate) and intangible assets (patents, licenses, etc.) by a foreign entity. A foreign entity may be: a natural person who does not have Polish citizenship; a legal person with its registered office abroad; an organizational unit that is not a legal person with its registered office abroad.Working age - the age of ability to work, i.e. for men the age group 18-64 years, for women - 18-59 years.

metadata.name: 9.B.8 Entered entities with foreign capital participation per 10 thous.population
metadata.priority: Strong and intelligent economy strengthening the competitiveness of the region (Strengthening the mechanisms of innovation transfer in the economy and development of intelligent specializations)
metadata.definition: An entity registered and operating in Poland, in whose share capital a foreign entity has shares per 10,000 inhabitants.
metadata.methodology: Foreign capital is capital contributed to an entity in the form of financial resources (cash, shares, bonds), tangible fixed assets (machinery, equipment, means of transport, real estate) and intangible assets (patents, licenses, etc.) by a foreign entity. A foreign entity may be: a natural person who does not have Polish citizenship; a legal person with its registered office abroad; an organizational unit that is not a legal person with its registered office abroad.Entity with foreign capital - an entity registered and operating in Poland, in whose share capital a foreign entity has shares (regardless of the size of this share).

metadata.name: 9.B.9 Outlays on recycling and waste utilization
metadata.priority: Strong and intelligent economy strengthening the competitiveness of the region (Strengthening the mechanisms of innovation transfer in the economy and development of intelligent specializations)
metadata.definition: Outlays for recycling and the use of waste.
metadata.methodology: Recycling - recovery, in which waste is reprocessed into products, materials or substances used for their original purpose or other purposes; this includes the reprocessing of organic material (organic recycling) but does not include energy recovery and reprocessing into materials that are to be used as fuels or for backfilling purposes.Capital expenditure - financial or material expenditure aimed at creating new fixed assets or improving (reconstruction, extension, reconstruction or modernization) of existing fixed assets, as well as expenditure on the so-called first investment equipment.

metadata.name: 9.C.1 Length of public roads with hard improved surface per 10 thous. inhabitants
metadata.priority: Strengthening the availability of transport infrastructure and the coherence of public communication in the region
metadata.definition: Length of public roads with hard surfaces per 10,000 inhabitants.
metadata.methodology: The basis for calculating the indicator is the total length of public roads with a hard surface as of 31 December.Public roads are roads that can be used by anyone in accordance with their intended use, with an appropriate category established in accordance with the provisions of the Act of 21 March 1985 on public roads (consolidated text Journal of Laws 2015 item 460). Due to their functions in the road network, public roads are divided into the following categories:- national roads,- provincial roads,- district roads,- municipal roads.Roads with an improved hard surface include roads with a surface made of stone cubes, clinker, concrete, stone-concrete slabs and bitumen.

metadata.name: 9.C.10 Number of bus station adjusted to carrying the disabled persons
metadata.priority: Strengthening the availability of transport infrastructure and the coherence of public communication in the region
metadata.definition: Number of bus stations adapted to serve disabled people (in out-of-town transport).
metadata.methodology: Bus station adapted to serve disabled people - a building object together with related construction equipment providing the necessary conditions for use by disabled people, in particular those using wheelchairs, e.g. equipped with facilities for access and exit from/to buildings, lifting equipment, stairs and ramps (Act of 7 July 1994 - Construction Law, Journal of Laws of 2017, item 1332, as amended) and the regulation of the Minister of Infrastructure of 12 April 2002 on the technical conditions that buildings and their location should meet (Journal of Laws of 2015, item 1422).Data comes from the statistical report T-06 report on passenger road transport. They concern the entities' own facilities (or managed by the carrier), conducting passenger transport in urban, intercity or international areas (full observation method).

metadata.name: 9.C.11 Share of urban transport stock adjusted for carrying disabled persons in urban total stock
metadata.priority: Strengthening the availability of transport infrastructure and the coherence of public communication in the region
metadata.definition: Share of urban transport stock adjusted for carrying disabled persons in urban total stock.
metadata.methodology: Applies to ground urban transport. Including enterprises and municipal transport services entities employing more than 9 persons. Moreover the urban services were conducted in all voivodships by enterprises providing interurban traffic.As of 31 December.

metadata.name: 9.C.2 Density of expressways and motorways per 1000 km²
metadata.priority: Strengthening the availability of transport infrastructure and the coherence of public communication in the region
metadata.definition: Percentage of persons in households living below relative poverty threshold which is determined at 50% of the mean expenditure at the level of all households.
metadata.methodology: The basis for calculating the indicator is the total length of express-ways and motorways as of 31 December. In accordance with the provisions of the Act of 21 March 1985 on public roads (uniform text, Journal of Laws 2015 item 460): a) an expressway is a road specially designed for motor vehicle traffic and which: – is provided with one or two carriageways, – has multi-level crossings with any road, railway or tramway track, footpath or waterway, with rare exceptions when one-level intersections with public roads are allowed, – is equipped with devices intended for serving the travelers, vehicles and packages, and reserved only for motorway users. b) a motorway is a road specially designed for motor vehicle traffic and which: – is provided with at least two permanently separated from each other carriageways for the opposite directions of traffic, – has multi-level crossings with any road, railway or tramway track, footpath or waterway, – is equipped with devices intended for serving the travelers, vehicles and packages, and reserved only for motorway users.

metadata.name: 9.C.3 Length of railway lines total per 100 km2
metadata.priority: Strengthening the availability of transport infrastructure and the coherence of public communication in the region
metadata.definition: Length of railway lines in operation per 100 km2
metadata.methodology: Length of railway lines operated - the sum of the construction lengths of standard gauge, narrow gauge and broad gauge railway lines in operation on 31 December. The construction length of railway lines is understood as the distance between its end points measured on single-track lines - along the track axis, and on multi-track lines along the axis of the longest track. Additional methodological explanations: 1. Standard gauge railway lines are lines whose tracks have a clearance of 1435 mm. 2. Narrow gauge railway lines are lines whose tracks have a clearance of less than 1435 mm. (e.g. 1000 mm, 785 mm). 3. Broad gauge railway lines are lines whose tracks have a clearance of greater than 1435 mm. (e.g. 1524 mm).

metadata.name: 9.C.4 Share of the length of electified operated railway lines in total length of operated railway lines
metadata.priority: Strengthening the availability of transport infrastructure and the coherence of public communication in the region
metadata.definition: The basis for calculating the indicator is the total length of electrified railway lines in operation on 31 December.
metadata.methodology: A railway line is an element of the railway network between two junction (or terminal) stations.The length of railway lines in operation is the sum of the construction lengths of standard gauge, narrow gauge and broad gauge railway lines. The construction length of railway lines is understood as the distance between their terminal points measured on single-track lines - along the track axis, and on multi-track lines - along the axis of the longest track.

metadata.name: 9.C.5 Transport of passengers per 1 inhabitant of urban areas
metadata.priority: Strengthening the availability of transport infrastructure and the coherence of public communication in the region
metadata.definition: The ratio of the number of passenger transport by public transport to the number of population in the cities.
metadata.methodology: Transport of passengers by public transport is the sum of passengers transported by public transport, generally accessible to the public, i.e. buses, trams and trolleybuses. A single pass is considered a transported passenger by public transport. The number of passengers is estimated on the basis of the number of one-off and multi-trip tickets sold, taking into account the standards for the number of journeys for a given multi-pass ticket accepted by public transport establishments. Data refer to enterprises and municipal transport companies employing more than 9 people. Data on residents of urban areas were taken from statistical information on the number of population in cities according to the actual place of residence.

metadata.name: 9.C.6 Percentage of buses adjusted to carrying the disabled persons in urban transport
metadata.priority: Strengthening the availability of transport infrastructure and the coherence of public communication in the region
metadata.definition: Percentage ratio of the number of buses adapted to transport disabled people to the total number of buses in public transport (as of December 31).
metadata.methodology: Bus adapted for the transport of disabled persons - in accordance with Regulation No. 107 of the UN ECE (OJ EU L 153 of 18.06.2015 - Uniform provisions concerning the approval of vehicles of category M2 or M3 with regard to their general construction) all single-deck, double-deck, rigid or articulated vehicles must allow access for people with reduced mobility, including at least one wheelchair user, as well as doors with a lift or ramp providing access for people using wheelchairs.The data concerns bus transport (including express buses) in cities - including the service of suburban and intercity traffic remaining in the hands of public transport companies and private companies serving certain cities or specific lines.The data comes from the statistical report T-06 report on passenger road transport and covers the rolling stock of entities conducting municipal passenger transport in the scope of urban transport, including the service of suburban areas (full observation method).

metadata.name: 9.C.7 Lenght of bicycle tracks per 100 km²
metadata.priority: Strengthening the availability of transport infrastructure and the coherence of public communication in the region
metadata.definition: Total length of bicycle lanes (bicycle tracks) per 100 km² total area in kilometers.
metadata.methodology: The bicycle tracks (bicycle lanes) is a road or a part of it that is designated for the use of bicycles and marked with appropriate road signs; is separated from other roads or roadways of the same road construction or by means of traffic safety equipment.

metadata.name: 9.C.8 Population density of the built-up and urbanized area (person/km2)
metadata.priority: Strengthening the availability of transport infrastructure and the coherence of public communication in the region
metadata.definition: The indicator is calculated based on the population status as of mid-year, i.e. 30.06, and on data on the geodetic area of the country according to directions of use.
metadata.methodology: Geodetic Area of the Country - Information on the geodetic status and directions of use of the country's surface based on the land and building records kept by the Head Office of Geodesy and Cartography (as of January 1).Population size - the number of people (total or according to specific features) residing in a given territory at a given time (as of January 1) resulting from the population balance.The most commonly used status is:1) at the end of each year, i.e. on December 31,2) at mid-year, i.e. on June 30. The average population status in the year under review is the status at mid-year. The average population status can also be calculated as the arithmetic mean of the population status at the beginning and end of the period under review (in practice, the status at the beginning of the period is the status at the end of the previous period).

metadata.name: 9.C.9 Number of railway station adjusted to carrying the disabled persons
metadata.priority: Strengthening the availability of transport infrastructure and the coherence of public communication in the region
metadata.definition: Number of railway stations adapted to serve disabled people.
metadata.methodology: Railway station adapted to serve disabled people – a building structure together with related construction equipment that provides the necessary conditions for use by disabled people, in particular those using wheelchairs, e.g. equipped with facilities for access to and exit from buildings, lifting equipment, stairs and ramps (Act of 7 July 1994 – Building Law, Journal of Laws of 2017, item 1332, as amended) and the Regulation of the Minister of Infrastructure of 12 April 2002 on the technical conditions that should be met by buildings and their location (Journal of Laws of 2015, item 1422). In practice, it is a facility with facilities for the movement of disabled people within the station (e.g. assistance service, sign language service, common ticket offices for all carriers, devices supporting movement with limited mobility, adapted toilets), platforms, underpasses, footbridges and other communication routes (e.g. lifts, platforms, horizontal markings).

metadata.name: 9.D.1 Percentage of individuals using the Internet regularly
metadata.priority: Increasing the availability of ICT services in the region
metadata.definition: Percentage of persons aged 16-74 using the Internet at least once a week.
metadata.methodology: Internet - global and public system of interconnected computer networks that use the standardized Internet Protocol Suite (TCP/IP). It is a network of networks that consists of millions of local networks and single computers from all other the world. E-mail, www, FTP and other services are available to use via Internet.Data comes from the survey “Information society indicators”, which is a questionnaire based sample survey carried out with use of a face-to face interview, in which participation is voluntary. The survey is conducted in April every year. In accordance with the requirements set up in the European Commission regulations, the survey covers households in the whole country (with at least one person aged 16-74) and all persons aged 16-74 living in those households. The survey excludes persons living in collective accommodation establishments such as student dormitories, employee hostels, social welfare homes, convents, hospitals, barracks, prisons etc. Foreigners may participate in the survey as long as they be able to speak Polish.

metadata.name: 9.D.2 Percentage of companies receiving orders via computer networks
metadata.priority: Increasing the availability of ICT services in the region
metadata.definition: Share of the number of enterprises carrying out e-sale through websites or EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) messages in the total number of active enterprises.
metadata.methodology: Sale via websites includes on-line orders placed in on-line stores or through electronic forms placed on a website or extranet regardless of the way of access (e.g. personal computer, laptop, mobile phone).Sale via EDI system is performed by messages of EDI type enabling creation of conditions for sending or receiving business information in an agreed format which allows its automatic processing e.g. EDIFACT, UBL, XML.Electronic Data Interchange is understood as sending or/and receiving messages (e.g. orders, invoices, payment transactions, product descriptions, transport documents, tax declarations) where: - it is carried out with enterprises, public administration authorities, financial institutions in agreed or standardized format which allows automatic processing e.g. EDI, EDIFACT, ODETTE, TRADACOMS, XML, xCBL, cXML, ebXML; - it excludes manually entered messages sent by electronic mail; - it is carried out through computer networks.Orders made by e-mail, fax or phone are excluded from e-trade transactions.

metadata.name: 9.D.3 Percentage of enterprises providing their employees with portable devices (e.g., laptops, smartphones) enabling mobile Internet access
metadata.priority: Increasing the availability of ICT services in the region
metadata.definition: Percentage of the number of employees equipping their employees with mobile devices in relation to the total number of enterprises.
metadata.methodology: The employed include people working under an employment contract and owners.Mobile devices include smartphones, laptops, notebooks, netbooks, tablets used by employees for business purposes. These devices can connect to the Internet via mobile networks.The source of data is the annual Survey on the Use of Information and Communication Technologies in Enterprises (form symbol SSI-01). The scope of the survey includes entities employing 10 or more people, which conduct business activities classified according to the Polish Classification of Activities (PKD) 2007 in selected sections:• Section C Industrial processing• Section D Production and supply of electricity, gas, steam and hot water• Section E Water supply, sewage and waste management; remediation• Section F Construction• Section G Trade; motor vehicle repair• Section H Transport and storage• Section I Accommodation and catering• Section J Information and communication• Section K Financial and insurance activities• Section L Real estate services• Section M Professional, scientific and technical activities• Section N Administration and support activities• Group 95.1 of Section S Repair and maintenance of computers and communication equipment

metadata.name: 9.D.4 Percentage of individuals using the Internet for contacts with public authorities or public services for submitting completed forms
metadata.priority: Increasing the availability of ICT services in the region
metadata.definition: Percentage of disabled individuals using the Internet for private contacts with public authorities (in the last 12 months).
metadata.methodology: Data comes from the survey “Information society indicators”, which is a questionnaire based sample survey carried out with use of a face-to face interview, in which participation is voluntary. The survey is conducted in April every year. In accordance with the requirements set up in the European Commission regulations, the survey covers households in the whole country (with at least one person aged 16-74) and all persons aged 16-74 living in those households. The survey excludes persons living in collective accommodation establishments such as student dormitories, employee hostels, social welfare homes, convents, hospitals, barracks, prisons etc. Foreigners may participate in the survey as long as they be able to speak Polish.The term of “public authorities” refers to authorities providing public services, as well as conducting typical administrative activities. This includes, for example, the tax authorities, customs authorities, registration authorities, social security institutions, public health authorities (eg. the National Health Fund, hospitals), institutions of environmental protection, and other authorities  providing  public activities. There are local, regional, and national public authorities.Contact with the public authorities via the Internet includes for example: search for information on web pages, downloading official forms, sending filled forms (excluding own-written e-mails).

metadata.name: 9.D.5 Percentage of households with broadband Internet access at home
metadata.priority: Increasing the availability of ICT services in the region
metadata.definition: Percentage of households with at least one person aged 16-74 having broadband Internet access at home.
metadata.methodology: Broadband Internet access is a connection that is characterized by a high speed of information flow measured in hundreds of kilobits per second or in megabits per second. Broadband access is enabled by technologies from the DSL family: ADSL, SDSL, etc., cable TV networks, satellite links, wireless connections via 3G modem or 3G handset.Data comes from the survey Information society indicators, which is a questionnaire based sample survey carried out with use of a face-to face interview, in which participation is voluntary.The survey is conducted in April every year. In accordance with the requirements set up in the Commission regulations, the survey covers all households in the country (with at least one person aged 16-74) and all persons aged 16-74 living in those households.The survey excludes persons living in collective accommodation establishments such as student dormitories, employee hostels, social welfare homes, convents, hospitals, barracks, prisons etc.) Foreigners may participate in the survey as long as they be able to speak Polish.Widespread and affordable broadband access is one of the ways of promoting knowledge and information based society. Prevalence of broadband Internet access is considered as a key indicator for policy making in the field of ICT. Broadband Internet connections allow transmission of high quality images, videos, watching TV or playing games on the Internet, phone calls over the Internet with the possibility of viewing the caller as well as allow the use of a variety of advanced Internet services.

metadata.name: 9.D.6 Percentage of individuals using the Internet for finding information about goods and services
metadata.priority: Increasing the availability of ICT services in the region
metadata.definition: Percentage of individuals aged 16-74 using the Internet for the finding information about goods and services (in the last 3 months).
metadata.methodology: Data comes from the survey “Information society indicators”, which is a questionnaire based sample survey carried out with use of a face-to face interview, in which participation is voluntary.The survey is conducted in April every year. In accordance with the requirements set up in the European Commission regulations, the survey covers households in the whole country (with at least one person aged 16-74) and all persons aged 16-74 living in those households.The survey excludes persons living in collective accommodation establishments such as student dormitories, employee hostels, social welfare homes, convents, hospitals, barracks, prisons etc. Foreigners may participate in the survey as long as they be able to speak Polish.

metadata.name: 10.A.1 Number of foundations, associations and social organizations recorded in the REGON register per 10 thous. population
metadata.goal: Goal 10. Reduce inequality within and among countries
metadata.priority: Combating social and economic exclusion, in particular through actions aimed at building the development of social capital
metadata.definition: The number of entities of the national economy - foundations, associations and social organizations recorded in the REGON register per 10000 inhabitants.
metadata.methodology: A foundation – a legal person whose activity is based on assets transferred by a founder and on current payments (subsidies), which is established for realization of socially or economically useful purposes compatible with the fundamental interests of the Republic of Poland, in particular such as: health care, development of economy and science, education, culture and art, human health and social work activities, environmental protection and monument preservation. Foundation acts under the Law of 6 April 1984 on foundations and on the basis of its statute. It acquires legal entity with being recorded in the National Court Register.A founder – a natural person irrespective of their nationality and place of residence, or a legal person with its seat in Poland or abroad.An association – a voluntary, self-governing, stable union of people for non-profit purposes, which independently determines its goals, activity programs and organizational structures, as well as adopts internal rules regarding its activities. Polish citizens with full legal capacity and public rights have the right to form associations. The legal basis for activities is the Law of 7 April 1989 on associations and an adopted statute. An association acquires legal entity and can start its activities with being recorded in the National Court Register. A simplified form of association is an ordinary association which does not have legal entity.REGON (National Official Register of Economic Entities) is a set of information on entities of national economy run in the computer system in the form of central and local databases. Currently, it is the only integrated register in Poland which involves all entities of national economy. Every entity has a unique nine-digit identification number (REGON) given by the Central Statistical Office, which is used in banking, customs, tax systems as well as in processing of data on foreign trade and in other commercial and marketing systems.According to the Act of 29 June 1995 on public statistics, every entity of the national economy is required to register its business activity in the REGON.

metadata.name: 10.A.2 Average monthly gross wages and salaries in the national economy (Poland = 100)
metadata.goal: Goal 10. Reduce inequality within and among countries
metadata.priority: Combating social and economic exclusion, in particular through actions aimed at building the development of social capital
metadata.definition: Average monthly gross wages and salaries in relation to the average value for the country.
metadata.methodology: Average monthly gross wages and salaries in the national economy is the relation of the sum of gross salaries and fees paid to certain groups of employees for performing work under labour contracts, payments from profit and from balance surplus in co-operatives, annual extra wages and salaries for employees of budgetary sphere entities to the average number of employees, excluding outworkers, apprentices and persons employed abroad.Data shall be provided together with personal income tax advances and compulsory social security contributions (pension, disability and sickness) paid by the insured employee.The data concern national economy with 10 or more employed persons  and budgetary sphere regardless of the number of employed persons.

metadata.name: 10.A.3 At-risk of poverty rate after social transfers
metadata.goal: Goal 10. Reduce inequality within and among countries
metadata.priority: Combating social and economic exclusion, in particular through actions aimed at building the development of social capital
metadata.definition: Percentage of persons with an equivalised disposable income (after social transfers) below the at-risk-of-poverty threshold set at 60% of the national median of equivalised disposable income.
metadata.methodology: The indicator is calculated on the basis of information obtained from the EU-SILC survey.EU-SILC survey (European Union Statistics on Income and Living Conditions) is a constant survey (conducted every year) whose subject are households and persons aged 16 and more in households. The survey is a panel study, i.e.  selected group of respondents is subject to several rounds of “observation” at intervals, so that it allows to observe and analyze the changing situation, attitudes, behaviour or opinions of a surveyed group.  Survey is conducted by face-to-face interview techniqueusing 2 questionnaires; one of which is used to obtain data on households, and the second to obtain data on individuals.The purpose of EU-SILC survey is to obtain information which allows the assessment of living conditions of Polish society and allows to compare them to the living conditions in other countries of European Union. This is achieved by adoption of a uniform methodology by Eurostat.At current requests of European Union authorities, module surveys devoted to selected issue are also conducted within EU-SILC survey (that is an additional questionnaire proceeded together with the basic survey).Central Statistical Office of Poland implemented EU-SILC survey in 2005.The survey unit is a household, which is understood as the persons who may be related or unrelated, living together and maintaining themselves jointly (multi-person household). Household can also be formed by one individual maintaining himself/herself  independently, regardless of  whether the individual lives alone or with other persons (one-person household).The following poverty thresholds are used for calculating the poverty scope:• relative poverty threshold – 50% of the mean expenditure determined at the level of all households,• “legal” poverty threshold – income per 1 person in household which, according to the Law on Social Assistance, provides eligibility for a monetary benefit from social assistance,• extreme poverty threshold – the subsistence minimum estimated by Institute of Labour and Social Studies (IPiSS), the subsistence minimum covers only those needs which satisfying cannot be postponed and consumption below that level leads to a biological destruction.In order to eliminate the impact of the socio-demographic composition of household on the maintenance costs, the so-called original equivalence scale has been used for the calculation of the households’ expenditure level and determination of poverty thresholds (relative and subsistence minimum).According to this scale:• weight 1 is attributed to the first person in the household at the age of 14 and over,• weight 0,7 — to any other person at that age,• weight 0,5 — to every child under 14.And thus, for instance, the relative poverty threshold for a 4-person household consisting of two adult persons and two children is 2,7 times as high as that for a 1-person household.

metadata.name: 10.A.4 Share of beneficiares from community social assistance in the total population
metadata.goal: Goal 10. Reduce inequality within and among countries
metadata.priority: Combating social and economic exclusion, in particular through actions aimed at building the development of social capital
metadata.definition: The ratio of the number of people receiving social assistance in the reference year to the total population of the territorial unit (as of 31 of December of the reference year).
metadata.methodology: Beneficiares from community social assistance (alternative name: using social welfare) are all members of households with at least one social assistance beneficiary and homeless people, who in the survey year received at least once any benefit provided by social welfare centers.The reason for this approach is the fact that when considering applications for social assistance, the material situation of all household members of the person submitting the application is analyzed and the income per capita in the household is calculated. In addition, it was assumed that every form of help indirectly, even a small impact on the living conditions of all persons remaining in the common household.
