Poor beneficiaries of social assistance provided in the place of residence are people whose income as a person in a single-person household or as a homeless person, and in the case of multi-person households - income per person in the household, it did not exceed the income criterion. The population of beneficiaries also includes homeless people benefiting from the help of municipalities.

metadata.name: 10.A.5 Percentage of households with broadband Internet access at home
metadata.goal: Goal 10. Reduce inequality within and among countries
metadata.priority: Combating social and economic exclusion, in particular through actions aimed at building the development of social capital
metadata.definition: Percentage of households with at least one person aged 16-74 having broadband Internet access at home.
metadata.methodology: Broadband Internet access is a connection that is characterized by a high speed of information flow measured in hundreds of kilobits per second or in megabits per second. Broadband access is enabled by technologies from the DSL family: ADSL, SDSL, etc., cable TV networks, satellite links, wireless connections via 3G modem or 3G handset.Data comes from the survey Information society indicators, which is a questionnaire based sample survey carried out with use of a face-to face interview, in which participation is voluntary.The survey is conducted in April every year. In accordance with the requirements set up in the Commission regulations, the survey covers all households in the country (with at least one person aged 16-74) and all persons aged 16-74 living in those households.The survey excludes persons living in collective accommodation establishments such as student dormitories, employee hostels, social welfare homes, convents, hospitals, barracks, prisons etc.) Foreigners may participate in the survey as long as they be able to speak Polish.Widespread and affordable broadband access is one of the ways of promoting knowledge and information based society. Prevalence of broadband Internet access is considered as a key indicator for policy making in the field of ICT. Broadband Internet connections allow transmission of high quality images, videos, watching TV or playing games on the Internet, phone calls over the Internet with the possibility of viewing the caller as well as allow the use of a variety of advanced Internet services.

metadata.name: 10.A.6 Percentage of individuals using the Internet for contacts with public authorities or public services for submitting completed forms
metadata.goal: Goal 10. Reduce inequality within and among countries
metadata.priority: Combating social and economic exclusion, in particular through actions aimed at building the development of social capital
metadata.definition: Percentage of disabled individuals using the Internet for private contacts with public authorities (in the last 12 months).
metadata.methodology: Data comes from the survey “Information society indicators”, which is a questionnaire based sample survey carried out with use of a face-to face interview, in which participation is voluntary. The survey is conducted in April every year. In accordance with the requirements set up in the European Commission regulations, the survey covers households in the whole country (with at least one person aged 16-74) and all persons aged 16-74 living in those households. The survey excludes persons living in collective accommodation establishments such as student dormitories, employee hostels, social welfare homes, convents, hospitals, barracks, prisons etc. Foreigners may participate in the survey as long as they be able to speak Polish.The term of “public authorities” refers to authorities providing public services, as well as conducting typical administrative activities. This includes, for example, the tax authorities, customs authorities, registration authorities, social security institutions, public health authorities (eg. the National Health Fund, hospitals), institutions of environmental protection, and other authorities  providing  public activities. There are local, regional, and national public authorities.Contact with the public authorities via the Internet includes for example: search for information on web pages, downloading official forms, sending filled forms (excluding own-written e-mails).

metadata.name: 10.A.7 Activity rate aged 15-89
metadata.goal: Goal 10. Reduce inequality within and among countries
metadata.priority: Combating social and economic exclusion, in particular through actions aimed at building the development of social capital
metadata.definition: The share of economically active persons (total or of given category) in the total of population or population of given category (in %). Average data per year.
metadata.methodology: Data comes from a sample survey: Labour Force Survey (LFS). The survey is conducted on a quarterly basis, using continuous observation (movable survey week) what allows presenting the situation on the labour market during a whole quarter. The survey covers persons aged 15-89 who are members of households in dwellings selected on a random basis. Outside the scope of the survey are members of households staying (the total duration of actual and planned stay is taken into account) 12 months or more abroad and in collective households (such as staff hotels, dormitories, boarding schools, nursing homes, etc.) and homeless people. In the context of economic activity - work is the main criterion in dividing the population, i.e. performing, holding or seeking work. According to the international standards, the tree main categories are distinguished: employed, unemployed and economically inactive persons.The employed and unemployed are the economically active population. The employed are defined as all persons aged 15-89 who during the reference week:  - performed for at least one hour any work generating pay or income, i.e. were employed by a public company/institution or private employer,, worked on their own (or leased) agricultural farm, or conducted their own economic activity outside agriculture, assisted (without pay) in work on family agricultural farm or in conducting family economic activity outside agriculture, - had work but did not perform it due to:   • sickness, or rest leave, leave related to parenthood (maternity, parental, paternity or childcare), organization of working time (work system or overtime receipt), job-related training, • the seasonal nature of the work, if they continued to regularly fulfill work or business-related tasks and duties (excluding legal or administrative duties) during the off-season, • other reasons, if the expected period of absence from work does not exceed 3 months - did not exceed 3 months.  According to international standards, among employees are also included persons performing outwork and apprentices with whom enterprises or natural persons signed a contract for occupational training or learning skills for a particular job (if they receive a payment). The employed do not include: volunteers and unpaid interns, people working in individual agriculture engaged in the production of agricultural products exclusively or primarily for their own consumption. Unemployed persons are persons aged 15-74 who simultaneously fulfil three conditions: - within the reference week were not employed, - were actively looking for work, i.e. for over 4 weeks (the reference week being the fourth one) had been involved in concrete actions aimed at finding a job, - were available to take up work within two weeks after the reference week. Persons who were not seeking work because they had already found a job, were only waiting to start work within the period no longer than 3 months, and were available for this job are also included in the category of the unemployed.

metadata.name: 10.A.8 Number of women who did not start work after childbirth per 10 thous. inhabitants
metadata.goal: Goal 10. Reduce inequality within and among countries
metadata.priority: Combating social and economic exclusion, in particular through actions aimed at building the development of social capital
metadata.definition: The number of women, who after giving a birth, registered in the respective labour office to find a job per 10 thousand of population.
metadata.methodology: The definition of registered unemployed persons is specified by the Law of 20 April 2004 on promoting employment and labour market institutions (Journal of Laws 2020 item 1409, with later amendments).An unemployed person is a person who is not employed or performing any other kind of paid work, available for full-time work (or in case of disabled persons — available for at least half of the full work-time required for a given occupation or service), are not attending any full-time school with the exception of schools for adults or taking an extra-curriculum exam in such schools or tertiary schools in the evening or weekend education system, and are registered in the powiat labour office respective to the person’s permanent or temporary place of residence, seeking employment or any other paid work if they, among others:1) is at least 18 years old and2) is less than 60 in case of women and less than 65 in case of men;3) did not become entitled to benefits from retirement pay or disability pension, family pension, education pension, social pension, does not receive pre-retirement benefit and allowance, rehabilitation benefit, sick benefit, maternity benefit or benefit of an amount equal to maternity benefit;4) is not an owner or a holder (independent or dependent) of an agricultural real estate with an area of agricultural land exceeding 2 convertible ha;5) is not eligible for retirement and disability insurance from constant work as a spouse or a household member on a farm with an area of agricultural land exceeding 2 convertible ha;6) does not have an entry in the register of economic activity or is not eligible –at the basis of separate legislation – for social insurance with an exception of farmers social insurance;7) is not a pre-trial detainee or a convicts serving punishment in prison;8) does not receive monthly income exceeding half of the monthly minimum wages and salaries for work excluding incomes received from interests or other incomes from cash kept on a bank accounts;9) does not receive permanent benefit on the basis of provisions of the regulations on social assistance;10) does not receive nursing benefit on the basis of provisions of the regulations on family allowances;11) does not receive training allowances after termination of employment.

metadata.name: 10.A.9 Employment rate of disabled persons aged 16-64
metadata.goal: Goal 10. Reduce inequality within and among countries
metadata.priority: Combating social and economic exclusion, in particular through actions aimed at building the development of social capital
metadata.definition: The share of employed disabled persons of given category in the total of disabled persons of that category (in %).
metadata.methodology: The data comes from the representative Labour Force Survey (LFS), conducted on a quarterly basis using the continuous observation method (moving week of the survey), allowing to illustrate the labour market situation over the entire quarter. The survey covers persons aged 15-89 who are members of households in drawn dwellings. Outside the scope of the survey, there are members of households staying (the total duration of actual and planned stay is taken into account) 12 months or more abroad and in collective households (such as staff hotels, dormitories, boarding schools, nursing homes, etc.) and homeless persons.In the context of economic activity – work is the main criterion in dividing the population, i.e. performing, holding or seeking work. According to the international standards, the tree main categories are distinguished: employed, unemployed and economically inactive persons. The employed, unemployed are the economically active population.Among the employed are included all persons aged 15-89 years who during the reference week:1) performed for at least 1 hour an income-generating or earning job, i.e., were employed by a public company/institution or private employer, worked on their own (or leased) agricultural farm or conducted their own economic activity outside agriculture, assisted (without pay) in running a family farm or family economic activity outside agriculture,2) had work, but did not perform it:- due to to sickness, vacation, parenthood-related leave (maternity leave, paternity or parental leave), working time arrangement (work system or compensation for overtime hours), training related to the performed work,- due to the seasonal nature of work, if they still regularly performed off-season work or business-related tasks and responsibilities (excluding legal or administrative obligations),- for other reasons, if the expected period of absence from work does not exceed 3 months.According to international standards, among employees are also included persons performing outwork and apprentices with whom enterprises or natural persons signed a contract for occupational training or learning skills for a particular job (if they receive a payment).Among the employed are not included: volunteers and unpaid interns, persons employed in private agriculture who produce agricultural exclusively or primarily for their own use.Population of disabled persons was legally separated from the total population aged 16-89, based on the legal criterion. Persons with disabilities include persons who are 16 years of age and in accordance with the provisions of the Act of 27 August 1997 on vocational and social rehabilitation and employment of disabled persons (Journal of Laws of 2016, item 2046, as amended). d.) have a disability degree certificate or an equivalent certificate:- significant degree of disability - this includes persons with the 1st disability group, or a certificate of total inability to work and live independently, or a certificate of permanent or long-term incapacity to work on a farm with the right to a care allowance,- moderate degree of disability - this includes people with disability group II or a certificate of total inability to work,- slight degree of disability - this includes persons with disability group III, or a certificate of partial inability to work and the advisability of retraining, or a certificate of permanent or long-term incapacity to work on a farm without entitlement to care allowance.

metadata.name: 11.A.1 Total expenditures of gminas and powiats on public safety and fire protection per capita
metadata.priority: Safe community
metadata.definition: Total expenditure of gminas and powiats dedicated to public safety and fire protection (i.e.  expenditure in Division 754 of budgetary classification) - per capita.
metadata.methodology: Information on expenditure of self government entities is based on reports  of Ministry of Finance.The following categories are included in local self-government entities: gminas (excluding gminas possessing city status with powiat status,) cities with powiat status, powiats, voivodships.Budgetary expenses of local self-government entities are grouped according to the principles set forth in the Act of Public Finances, Act on Gmina Self-government, Act on Powiat Self-government, Act on Voivodship and Self-government and Local Government Finance Act. The expenses are grouped by divisions and chapters (defining types of activity) and paragraphs (defining types of expenditure). Detailed classification of expenditure is determined by the Minister of Finance under the subsequent regulations.Among types of expenditure, current expenditure and property expenditure (including investments) can be distinguished. Current expenditure includes allocations, benefits for natural person, current expenditure of budgetary entities (including wages and salaries, wages and salaries related expenditures, purchase of goods and services).Among kinds of activities, according to divisions and chapters of the budgetary classification - among others, public safety and fire protection (Division 754) is identifiied.

metadata.name: 11.A.2 Total expenditures of gminas on climate and air protection per capita
metadata.priority: Safe community
metadata.definition: The sum of expenditures of municipalities (including cities with county status) on air protection and climate in terms per capita (as of 30 June  of the surveyed year).
metadata.methodology: Expenditure on climate and air protection per capita includes expenditure of municipalities budgets (of which municipalities as cities with county status) included in division 90005 „Protection of air and climate” of the Budget Classification.The indicators relating to the number and structure of the population (sex, age groups) since 200 have been recalculated according to the balance prepared on the basis based on the National Census 2021. Indicators related to the number and structure of the population (sex, age groups) from 2020 have been recalculated according to the balance prepared based on the results of the National Census 2021.

metadata.name: 11.A.3 Share of municipally waste collected seperately in relation to the total municipal waste
metadata.priority: Safe community
metadata.definition: Mass of municipal waste collected separately to total municipal waste collected within a year.
metadata.methodology: Municipal wastes are defined as the wastes generated in households (excluding end of life vehicles) as well as waste which does not contain hazardous substances, coming from other waste producers, which in their nature or composition are similar to wastes generated in households; mixed municipal waste remain mixed municipal waste, even if they have been processed, which did not change substantially their attributes.Collection of wastes is gathering the wastes before their transportation to the places of processing, including preliminary sorting which does not lead to fundamental change of their character, composition and not resulting in change in the waste classification. It includes temporary waste storage.According to Waste Act selective collection of waste is a collection in which the waste stream, in order to facilitate a specific treatment, covers only the waste characterized by the same properties and the same characteristics.Data on municipal wastes are gathered from units responsible for wastes collection. Information comprise the group of wastes coded 20: Municipal waste together with selectively gathered fractions, and  wastes coded 15 01: Packaging waste (together with selectively gathered municipal waste) if come from municipal sector.

metadata.name: 11.B.1 Number of active bus and tram stops per 1 thous. population
metadata.priority: Improving communication conditions and accessibility
metadata.definition: Number of active bus (including trolleybus) and tram stops operating in the area of a given territorial unit per 1000 population (as of 31 of December the reference year).
metadata.methodology: A stop is a place where public transport vehicles stop, marked with appropriate road signs.Data concern to public transport stops. The number of stops includes:• bus and trolleybus stops;• tram stops;• common stops for buses and trams

metadata.name: 11.B.2 Lenght of bicycle tracks per 100 km²
metadata.priority: Improving communication conditions and accessibility
metadata.definition: Total length of bicycle lanes (bicycle tracks) per 100 km2 total area in kilometers.
metadata.methodology: The bicycle tracks (bicycle lanes) is a road or a part of it that is designated for the use of bicycles and marked with appropriate road signs; is separated from other roads or roadways of the same road construction or by means of traffic safety equipment.

metadata.name: 11.B.3 Transport of passengers per 1 inhabitant of urban areas
metadata.priority: Improving communication conditions and accessibility
metadata.definition: The ratio of the number of passenger transport by public transport to the number of population in the cities.
metadata.methodology: Transport of passengers by public transport is the sum of passengers transported by public transport, generally accessible to the public, i.e. buses, trams and trolleybuses. A single pass is considered a transported passenger by public transport. The number of passengers is estimated on the basis of the number of one-off and multi-trip tickets sold, taking into account the standards for the number of journeys for a given multi-pass ticket accepted by public transport establishments. Data refer to enterprises and municipal transport companies employing more than 9 people. Data on residents of urban areas were taken from statistical information on the number of population in cities according to the actual place of residence.

metadata.name: 11.B.4 Share of urban transport stock adapted for transporting people with disabilities in the total urban transport stock
metadata.priority: Improving communication conditions and accessibility
metadata.definition: Share of urban transport stock adjusted for carrying disabled persons in urban total stock.
metadata.methodology: Applies to ground urban transport. Including enterprises and municipal transport services entities employing more than 9 persons. Moreover the urban services were conducted in all voivodships by enterprises providing interurban traffic.As of 31 December.

metadata.name: 11.C.1 Total number of dwellings in gminas’ dwelling stocks
metadata.priority: Meeting the housing needs of the urban population by building a universal and accessible housing market
metadata.definition: The number of dwellings that are located in buildings in gminas’ dwelling stocks.
metadata.methodology: Gmina (municipal) stocks - dwellings owned by gminas or powiats (local self-government communities),owned by gminas but located in buildings constituting a shared real property e.j. dwellings which serve meeting the needs of all inhabitants of A gmina, as well as dwellings handed over to gminas, but remaining at the disposal of public use units, such as: units performing health care activities, social welfare centres, educational system entities, culture institutions, serving mainly the housing needs to employees of these entities.

metadata.name: 11.D.1 Area of landscape under legal protection per capita
metadata.priority: Access to green spaces and good quality public space
metadata.definition: Area of protected landscapes per capita.
metadata.methodology: Protected landscape area is an area protected due to particularly outstanding landscape features of the area of diversified ecosystems, valuable especially owing to the potential for satisfying needs connected with tourism and recreation, or owing to the existing or restored ecological corridors. Designation of protected landscape area by resolution of Voivodship sejmik (elected assembly).

metadata.name: 11.D.2 Area of street greenery per capita
metadata.priority: Access to green spaces and good quality public space
metadata.definition: The sum of the area of street greenery in a given territorial unit per inhabitant (as of 31 December of the reference year).
metadata.methodology: Street greenery is the greenery that accompanies roads in the building area.Data concers to street greenery, which is owned both by the municipalities and other units.

metadata.name: 11.D.3 Share of green areas in the total area
metadata.priority: Access to green spaces and good quality public space
metadata.definition: Share of green areas in the total area of Poland.
metadata.methodology: Green areas - areas including technical infrastructure and buildings functionally associated with them, covered with vegetation, located in the village of dense buildings or cities, used for the aesthetic, recreational, therapeutic or shielding purposes, in particular parks, lawns, promenades, boulevards, botanical and zoological gardens, children's playgrounds, historic garden, cemeteries, green areas located near roads in the build up areas, squares, historic fortifications, buildings, landfill sites, airports, railway stations and industrial buildings.

metadata.name: 11.E.1 Share of Local spatial management plans, the preparation of which takes more than 3 years
metadata.priority: Spatial cohesion of sustainable cities in the region
metadata.definition: Share of drafts of local spatial development plans that take more than 3 years to be drawn up in the total number of drafts of local spatial development plans.
metadata.methodology: A local spatial management plan – document outlining spatial plan of the municipality (planning document), adopted as a bye-law by the commune council. This document divides the land that it covers by use of dividing lines, into sub-areas with designated land functions, development principles, provision with infrastructure, and – if deemed necessary – local conditions, principles and standards for building design, as well as other conditions requiring planning regulations.
metadata.source: Ministry of Economic Development and Technology

metadata.name: 11.E.2 Urbanization rate
metadata.priority: Spatial cohesion of sustainable cities in the region
metadata.definition: The share of the population in cities in the total population.
metadata.methodology: Population in cities is the number of people living in localities with official city rights (city status). Data concern as of 30 June of the surveyed year.

metadata.name: 11.E.3 Share of area covered by local-land development plans in urban gminas
metadata.priority: Spatial cohesion of sustainable cities in the region
metadata.definition: Share of urban gminas’ area covered by applicable local spatial management plans in the total geodetic area in urban gminas.
metadata.methodology: Local spatial management plan is an act of local law determining destiny, terms of management and development of land, as well as arrangement of public investments. The plan consists of a description that is a text of the plan contained in resolution of the City Council and the drawing of the plan that is a graphical annex to the resolution. It is an optional document, i.e. gminas (communes) do not have the obligation to draw it up. The local plan shall mandatory include, among others: - use of land and the lines dividing areas for various land use; - the principles of environment, nature and cultural landscape protection; - the principles of cultural heritage, monuments and contemporary culture goods protection; - building lines, overall dimension of objects and indicators of build-up intensity; - the principles of modernization, expansion and building of communication systems and technical infrastructure. The consequence of the preparation of the local plan is an obligation to draw up documents such as economic impact forecast for adoption of local spatial management plan and environmental impact assessment.The solutions proposed in the plan may in fact significantly affect the transformation of the natural environment and the value of the properties. According to the law the content of the plan is divided into mandatory and optional part. The project of the plan is a subject of public consultations, requires a range of opinions and agreements to which act refers to. The geodetic area is the surface area of units of territorial division of the State according to the National Register of Boundaries.

metadata.name: 11.F.1 Share of gmina self-government carrying out revitalisation activities
metadata.priority: Revitalization of urban areas in the region
metadata.definition: Share of urban municipalities that carried out revitalisation activities in total urban municipalities in a given year.
metadata.methodology: Revitalization in the municipality - the process of deriving from crisis state degraded areas, conducted in comprehensive manner, through integrated activities for local community, space and economy, territorially focused, carried out by stakeholders of revitalisation, based on activities prepared by municipalities (municipal, commune) and conducted on the basis of a strategic document (municipalities revitalisation programme, revitalisation programme or another strategic document).

metadata.name: 12.A.1 Waste generated (excluding municipal waste) per capita
metadata.priority: Improving the quality and protection of the natural environment through rational waste management
metadata.definition: Total weight of waste generated during the surveyed year (excluding municipal waste) per capita (as of 30 June of the surveyed year).
metadata.methodology: The indicator is calculated on the basis of data from industrial plants generating  during the period of  one year summary over 1 thousand tons of hazardous waste and other wastes, excluding municipal waste or owning 1 mln tone and more of accumulated wastes.Waste generated is the amount of waste created in the process of carried activity or living, with determining the amount of wastes that has undergone the recovery process including disposing at landfills.
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 12.A.2 Area of uncontrolled dumping sites per 100 km² total area
metadata.priority: Improving the quality and protection of the natural environment through rational waste management
metadata.definition: The area of the existing wild dumps containing municipal waste inventoried by municipalities per 100km2 of total area.
metadata.methodology: The area of the existing illegal dumps shows the illegal dumps existing in the commune, ie the area of places not intended for waste storage, on which municipal waste is dumped, inventoried as of December 31.

metadata.name: 12.A.3 Municipal waste per capita
metadata.priority: Improving the quality and protection of the natural environment through rational waste management
metadata.definition: Total weight of municipal waste generated during the surveyed year per capita (as of  30 June of the surveyed year).
metadata.methodology: Municipal waste - waste generated in households, excluding end-of-life vehicles or generated by other waste producers (excluding hazardous waste) which on the account of its character and composition is similar to waste from households.
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 12.A.4 Reclaimed area of operational landfill sites on which municipal waste is treated
metadata.priority: Improving the quality and protection of the natural environment through rational waste management
metadata.definition: Area of active landfills where municipal waste is disposed of and which has been rehabilitated.
metadata.methodology: Recultivated (Reclamationed) waste landfill - waste landfill area, the exploitation of with has come to the end, and where the works targeted at creating and restoring its usability through proper relief of the land, enhancement of physical and chemical features as well as regulation of water conditions. Data concern as of 31 December of the surveyed year.
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 12.A.5 Number of plants generating waste
metadata.priority: Improving the quality and protection of the natural environment through rational waste management
metadata.definition: Number of plants generating waste as at 31 December of the year under review.
metadata.methodology: Data concern on plants generating waste are collected on the form „OS-6”. Entities that have generated more than 1 thousand tonnes of waste (excluding municipal waste) per municipality during the year or have 1 million tonnes or more of waste accumulated on their own are required to submit reports: landfills; in underground landfills; in extractive waste facilities (including heaps, sediment ponds).
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 12.A.6 Share of municipally waste collected separately in relation to the total municipal waste
metadata.priority: Improving the quality and protection of the natural environment through rational waste management
metadata.definition: Mass of municipal waste collected separately to total municipal waste collected within a year.
metadata.methodology: Municipal wastes are defined as the wastes generated in households (excluding end of life vehicles) as well as waste which does not contain hazardous substances, coming from other waste producers, which in their nature or composition are similar to wastes generated in households; mixed municipal waste remain mixed municipal waste, even if they have been processed, which did not change substantially their attributes.Collection of wastes is gathering the wastes before their transportation to the places of processing, including preliminary sorting which does not lead to fundamental change of their character, composition and not resulting in change in the waste classification. It includes temporary waste storage.According to Waste Act selective collection of waste is a collection in which the waste stream, in order to facilitate a specific treatment, covers only the waste characterized by the same properties and the same characteristics.Data on municipal wastes are gathered from units responsible for wastes collection. Information comprise the group of wastes coded 20: Municipal waste together with selectively gathered fractions, and  wastes coded 15 01: Packaging waste (together with selectively gathered municipal waste) if come from municipal sector.

metadata.name: 12.A.7 Share of waste recovered (excluding municipal waste) in waste generated during the year
metadata.priority: Improving the quality and protection of the natural environment through rational waste management
metadata.definition: Share of waste (excluding municipal waste) recovered in the amount of waste generated during the year.
metadata.methodology: Waste generated – the amount of waste generated as the result of economic or life activities.Waste recovery is any operation the principal result of which is waste serving a useful purpose by replacing other materials which would otherwise have been used to fulfil a particular function, or waste being prepared to fulfil that function, in the plant or in the wider economy.
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 12.B.1 Recycling level of preparation for reuse and recovery of other than hazardous construction and demolition waste that is municipal waste
metadata.priority: Supporting the development of a closed-loop economy model in order to rationally use resources and reduce the negative impact of manufactured products on the environment
metadata.definition: Percentage of non-hazardous construction and demolition waste that is municipal waste recycled, prepared for re-use or recovered.
metadata.methodology: Waste recovery is any operation the principal result of which is waste serving a useful purpose by replacing other materials which would otherwise have been used to fulfil a particular function, or waste being prepared to fulfil that function, in the plant or in the wider economy.Recycling - any recovery operation by which waste materials are reprocessed into products, materials or substances whether for the original or other purposes. It includes the reprocessing of organic material but does not include energy recovery and the reprocessing into materials that are to be used as fuels or for backfilling operations.Municipal waste - waste generated in households, excluding end-of-life vehicles or generated by other waste producers (excluding hazardous waste) which on the account of its character and composition is similar to waste from households.
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 12.B.2 Outlays on recycling and waste utilization
metadata.priority: Supporting the development of a closed-loop economy model in order to rationally use resources and reduce the negative impact of manufactured products on the environment
metadata.definition: Outlays for recycling and the use of waste.
metadata.methodology: Recycling - recovery, in which waste is reprocessed into products, materials or substances used for their original purpose or other purposes; this includes the reprocessing of organic material (organic recycling) but does not include energy recovery and reprocessing into materials that are to be used as fuels or for backfilling purposes.Capital expenditure - financial or material expenditure aimed at creating new fixed assets or improving (reconstruction, extension, reconstruction or modernization) of existing fixed assets, as well as expenditure on the so-called first investment equipment.

metadata.name: 12.C.1 Number of registered regional and traditional products
metadata.priority: Increasing the economic potential of regions and permanently improving their competitiveness by strengthening the production of traditional and regional products
metadata.definition: The total number of traditional products registered in the list of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development, established on the basis of the Act on the Registration and Protection of Names and Designations of Agricultural Products and Foodstuffs and on Traditional Products.
metadata.methodology: On the basis of the Act except the regulation on the registration of names at EU level, the Act also creates a List of Traditional Products. The list includes products whose quality or unique characteristics and properties result from the use of traditional production methods, which are part of the cultural heritage of the region in which they are produced and which are part of the identity of the local community. At the same time, traditional production methods used for at least 25 years are considered to be traditional. The Traditional Products List aims to disseminate information about products produced by traditional, historically well-established methods.
metadata.source: Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development

metadata.name: 12.D.1 Share of certified agricultural land area of organic farms in total agricultural land area of farms
metadata.priority: Improving the competitiveness of farms through the development of organic farming
metadata.definition: Agricultural certified land area of organic farms in relation to total agricultural land area of farms (in %).
metadata.methodology: Organic farm uses organic methods of agricultural production, and has a certificate granted to it by a certification body or is under conversion to organic methods of agricultural production under control of a certification body. The certification bodies for organic farming are bodies which, by the decision of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development, have been authorized to carrying out inspections and issuing and revoking a certificate of conformity with regard to organic agriculture (Law of 25 June 2009 on organic farming, Journal of Laws No. 116, item 975). Organic agricultural land is land where the organic methods of agricultural production are implemented. The area of certified organic agricultural land in organic holdings covers agricultural land area whose conversion to organic agriculture has been finished. The agricultural land area includes area of agricultural land in a good agricultural condition and other agricultural land. Agricultural land in a good agricultural condition - maintained in accordance with norms meeting requirements of the Law of 5 II 2015 on payments under direct support schemes (uniform text Journal of Laws 2015 item 1551) - include sown area, fallow land, permanent crops (including orchards), kitchen gardens, permanent meadows and permanent pastures. Other agricultural land is agricultural land which is not used and maintained in a good condition. Data on the total agricultural land area do not include land of owners of agricultural land who do not conduct agricultural activities, and land of owners of less than 1 ha of agricultural land who conduct agricultural activities on a small scale (below specific thresholds).
metadata.source: Chief Inspection of Agricultural and Food Quality / Statistics Poland
metadata.notes: In accordance with gradual changes in the methodology of agricultural surveys, introduced since 2010 in order to adjust it to EU standards and encompass changes taking place in the Polish agriculture, the definition of the agricultural farm has been changed. In accordance with the definition binding since 2010, agricultural surveys do not include owners of agricultural land who do not conduct agricultural activities, and owners of less than 1 ha of agricultural land who conduct agricultural activities on a small scale (below the specified thresholds).

metadata.name: 12.D.2 Number of organic farms (Poland = 100)
metadata.priority: Improving the competitiveness of farms through the development of organic farming
metadata.definition: The indicator presents the share of organic farms in the total number of organic farms in the country, calculated in %.
metadata.methodology: Organic farm uses organic methods of agricultural production which has a certificate granted to it by a certification body or which is under conversion to organic methods of agricultural production under control of a certification body.The certification bodies for organic farming are bodies which, by the decision of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development, have been authorized to carrying out inspections and issuing and revoking a certificate of conformity with regard to organic agriculture – Law dated 25th June 2009 on organic farming (uniform text Journal of Laws 2019, item 1353, 1824).
metadata.source: Chief Inspection of Agricultural and Food Quality / Statistics Poland

metadata.name: 12.D.3 Share of organic farm area in total agricultural land
metadata.priority: Improving the competitiveness of farms through the development of organic farming
metadata.definition: Area of organic agricultural land in organic farms in relation to the total agricultural land area of agricultural holdings.
metadata.methodology: Organic farm uses organic methods of agricultural production which has a certificate granted to it by a certification body or which is under conversion to organic methods of agricultural production under control of a certification body.The certification bodies for organic farming are bodies which, by the decision of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development, have been authorized to carrying out inspections and issuing and revoking a certificate of conformity with regard to organic agriculture – Law of 25 June 2009 on organic farming (uniform text Journal of Laws 2019, item 1353, 1824).Organic agricultural land is land where the organic methods of agricultural production are implemented. The area of certified organic agricultural land in organic holdings covers agricultural land area which conversion to organic agriculture was finished.The agricultural land area including area of agricultural land in a good agricultural condition and other agricultural land Agricultural land in a good agricultural condition – maintained in accordance with norms meeting requirements of the Law on payments under direct support schemes, dated 5th February 2015 (uniform text Journal of Laws 2018 item 1312, with later amendments) – include sown area, fallow land, permanent crops (including orchards), kitchen gardens, permanent meadows and permanent pastures. Other agricultural land is agricultural land which is not used and maintained in a good condition.Data on the total agricultural land area do not include land of owners of agricultural land who do not conduct agricultural activities, and land of owners of less than 1 ha of agricultural land who conduct agricultural activities on a small scale (below specific thresholds).

metadata.name: 12.E.1 Total consumption of electricity per capita
metadata.priority: Raising awareness and ecological education related to conscious consumption and limiting climate change
metadata.definition: Total electricity consumption in relation to number of inhabitants.
metadata.methodology: Electricity consumption is the amount of electricity delivered to domestic electricity market including: production (indigenous production) of the energy for own-account and electricity import, reduced by export and balance of domestic stocks.

metadata.name: 12.E.2 Total household water consumption per user
metadata.priority: Raising awareness and ecological education related to conscious consumption and limiting climate change
metadata.definition: Amount of water consumed for industry, agriculture, forestry purposes, filling and completing fishponds and exploitation of water supply network per capita.
metadata.methodology: Consumption of water - In the statistic it is the sum of water consumption for the purposes of: •	industry - water consumed for production, operational, social, administrative purposes (except for water supplied to residential buildings, eg. plant buildings) excluding water sold and losses in water network; •	agriculture and forestry - water consumed is water withdrawn for filling and completing fishponds; •	exploitation of water supply network - water delivered to households, individual farms, residents of lon-term collective accomodation facilities (dormitories, orphanages, nursing homes, monasteries etc.), entrepreneurs and manufacturing plants in all sectors of the national economy - whether it was used for production or social and living purposes (eg. in bathhouse, canteens, office buildings) •	and other purposes.

metadata.name: 13.A.1 Emissions of carbon dioxide from plants especially noxious to air purity per capita
metadata.priority: Effective reduction of CO₂ concentration in the atmosphere
metadata.definition: Total mass of carbon dioxide emitted per inhabitant from particularly burdensome plants in the surveyed year (as of 30 June of the surveyed year).
metadata.methodology: Plants particularly burdensome for air cleanliness are so-called point sources of pollutant emissions, which include all organizational units determined by the then Minister of Environmental Protection and Natural Resources on the basis of a certain amount of fees paid in 1986 for the annual emission of air pollutants at the rates specified in the Regulation of the Council of Ministers of 13 January 1986 on fees for the economic use of the environment and introducing changes thereto (Journal of Laws No. Journal of Laws No 7, item 40, as amended). The set of reporting entities (establishments) established in this way is maintained annually, which, among other things, ensures continuity and comparability of audit results. This grouping may be increased only in special cases, e.g. by units newly launched or extended with a high threshold scale of pollutant emissions.Emission of air pollutants - release of dust and gaseous pollutants into the atmosphere in the organized (through stationary point-sources) or unorganized manner (from dumps, landfills, during reloading of loose or volatile substances, through roof and window ventilation, due to forest fires, etc.).
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 13.B.1 Electricity generated from renewable sources
metadata.priority: Introduction of innovative technologies for the use of available energy sources
metadata.definition: Total mass of carbon dioxide emitted per inhabitant from particularly burdensome plants in the surveyed year (as of 30 June of the surveyed year).
metadata.methodology: Energy from renewable sources is the energy derived from natural, repetitive environmental processes, obtained from renewable non-fossil energy sources (energy: water, wind, solar heat, geothermal heat, waves, sea currents and tides and energy obtained from biomass, biogas, biofuels). In domestic conditions, energy from renewable sources comprises energy obtained from direct use of solar heat, wind, geothermal resources (from the Earth), water and energy generated from biomass, biogas, biofuels.Electricity gross generation - energy transformations output in the form of electrical current, as measured on generator contacts.
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 13.B.2 Outlays on fixed assets serving environmental protection in research-development activity
metadata.priority: Introduction of innovative technologies for the use of available energy sources
metadata.definition: Gross fixed capital formation for environmental protection in research and development.
metadata.methodology: Gross fixed capital formation – expenditure that increases the value of fixed assets and expenditure incurred for the repair of fixed assets and the increase in intangible assets.Investments on environmental protection – expenditure on creation or increase of fixed assets serving environmental protection. They can be expressed:1) in monetary terms, as investment expenditures incurred for environmental protection activities,2) in tangible form (effects), as environmental protection devices or technical equipment used for environmental protection purposes. Those may be end-of-pipe equipment or integrated technologies.Research and development (R&D) - research and experimental development (R&D) comprise creative and systematic work undertaken in order to increase the stock of knowledge - including knowledge of humankind, culture and society - and to devise new applications of available knowledge.The five criteria for identifying R&D:• novel - to be aimed at new findings,• creative - to be based on original, not obvious, concepts and hypotheses,• uncertain - to be uncertain about the final outcome and the cost, including time allocation,• systematic - to be conducted in a planned way (with identified the purpose of the R&D project and the sources of funding),• transferable and/or reproducible - to lead to results that could be possibly reproduced.Research and development includes:1. scientific researcha)	- basic researchb)	- applied research (industrial research)2. experimental development
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 13.C.1 The density of heating transmission and distribution network per 1 km²
metadata.priority: Strengthening the role of adaptation to climate change as a means of combating climate change equivalent to mitigation
metadata.definition: The sum of the transmission (main) thermic-line and distribution thermic-line per 1 km2 of total area.
metadata.methodology: Transmission (main) thermic-line - system of pipes supplying heating agent to distribution pipes.Distribution thermic-line - system of distribution pipes supplying heating factor to household connections.

metadata.name: 13.C.2 Emissions of air pollutants from plants especially noxious to air purity per 1 km²
metadata.priority: Strengthening the role of adaptation to climate change as a means of combating climate change equivalent to mitigation
metadata.definition: Total mass of particulate pollutants from particularly burdensome plants emitted in the surveyed year per 1 km² of area.
metadata.methodology: Plants particularly burdensome for air cleanliness are so-called point sources of pollutant emissions, which include all organizational units determined by the then Minister of Environmental Protection and Natural Resources on the basis of a certain amount of fees paid in 1986 for the annual emission of air pollutants at the rates specified in the Regulation of the Council of Ministers of 13 January 1986 on fees for the economic use of the environment and introducing changes thereto (Journal of Laws No. Journal of Laws No 7, item 40 as amended). The set of reporting entities (establishments) established in this way is maintained annually, which, among other things, ensures continuity and comparability of audit results. This grouping may be increased only in special cases, e.g. by units newly launched or extended with a high threshold scale of pollutant emissions.Particulate pollutant emissions – the introduction into the atmosphere of solid particles with macroscopic and colloidal fragmentation, the concentration of which exceeds the average content of these substances in clean air, adversely affecting human health and the state and quality of the environment.
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 13.C.3 Emissions of air pollutants-gases from plants especially noxious to air purity per 1 km²
metadata.priority: Strengthening the role of adaptation to climate change as a means of combating climate change equivalent to mitigation
metadata.definition: Total mass of gaseous pollutants from particularly arduous plants emitted in the surveyed year per 1 km² of surface area.
metadata.methodology: Plants particularly burdensome for air cleanliness are so-called point sources of pollutant emissions, which include all organizational units determined by the then Minister of Environmental Protection and Natural Resources on the basis of a certain amount of fees paid in 1986 for the annual emission of air pollutants at the rates specified in the Regulation of the Council of Ministers of 13 January 1986 on fees for the economic use of the environment and introducing changes thereto (Journal of Laws No. Journal of Laws No 7, item 40 as amended). The set of reporting entities (establishments) established in this way is maintained annually, which, among other things, ensures continuity and comparability of audit results. This grouping may be increased only in special cases, e.g. by units newly launched or extended with a high threshold scale of pollutant emissions.Emission of gaseous pollutants – the release into the atmosphere of gaseous substances the concentration of which exceeds the average content of these substances in clean air, adversely affecting human health and the state and quality of the environment.
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 13.C.4 Outlays on fixed assets and tangible effects of investments in environmental protection and water management per capita
metadata.priority: Strengthening the role of adaptation to climate change as a means of combating climate change equivalent to mitigation
metadata.definition: Outlays on fixed assets for environmental protection per capita.
metadata.methodology: Investment outlays - Financial or tangible outlays, the purpose of which is the creation of new fixed assets or the improvement (rebuilding, enlargement, reconstruction, modernization) of existing capital asset items, as well as outlays on so-called initial investments.Outlays on fixed assets include:- purchase of land (including permanent usufruct of land),- buildings, apartments and civil engineering constructions (including outlays on construction and installation works, design and cost estimation documentation),- technical equipment and machines, -means of transport,- tools, accessories, movable goods and equipment,- other fixed assets aimed at environmental protection and water management.From 1999, data on outlays on fixed assets for environmental protection and their tangible effects are presented in accordance with the Polish Statistical Classification concerning Activity and Equipment related to Environmental Protection introduced by the virtue of the regulation of the Council of Ministers of 2 March 1999 (O. J. No. 25, item 218). This classification was compiled on the basis of the ECE/UN Single European Standard Statistical Classification of Environmental Protection Activities and Facilities as well as the European System for the Collection of Economic Information on the Environment (SERIEE), implemented by the European Union (EUROSTAT).

metadata.name: 13.C.5 Outlays on fixed assets serving climate and air protection per capita
metadata.priority: Strengthening the role of adaptation to climate change as a means of combating climate change equivalent to mitigation
metadata.definition: Gross fixed capital formation for the protection of ambient air and climate per capita (as of 30 June of the surveyed year).
metadata.methodology: Gross fixed capital formation – expenditure that increases the value of fixed assets and expenditure incurred for the repair of fixed assets and the increase in intangible assets.Investments related to the protection of ambient air and climate – installations for cleansing and deodorising equipment (dust removal, reduction, disposal and neutralisation of gaseous pollutants) and installations applying chemical transformation reactions to substances less harmful to the environment, together with complete equipment and a set of necessary auxiliary devices ensuring the proper operation of the installation and equipment and apparatus ensuring the reduction of the amount or concentrations of pollutants arising or emitted, tasks related to equipping with air pollution control and measurement equipment.The investments also include: new fuel combustion techniques and technologies; modernisation of boiler and heating plants in order to reduce pollutants excreted into the air resulting from the combustion process; unconventional energy sources (e.g. wind farms, use of geothermal waters); adaptation of internal combustion engines to gaseous fuel, as well as the construction of a hydrocracking unit.Contaminant abatement devices, which are an integral part of the technological process ensuring the appropriate quality of raw materials and semi-finished products for subsequent production stages, are not included. This also applies to the installation of all kinds of auxiliary equipment necessary for technological or scientific reasons for the production plant.
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 13.D.1 Share of gaseous pollution retained or neutralized in pollutant reduction systems in the total pollutants generated
metadata.priority: Increasing the region's resilience to the effects of climate change
metadata.definition: The indicator is represented as a percentage ratio of the volume of retained pollution and the volume of produced pollution i.e. retained and emitted.
metadata.methodology: The value of this indicator is between 0 and 100%. The closer to 100% is the value, the bigger is the protection potential of a particular source of pollution.Data on the volume of contained or neutralized particulate pollution illustrate volume of pollution reduced in devices designed for protection of atmosphere, installed at plants of significant nuisance to air quality. Efficiency of cleaning devices determined as the level of pollution reduction is the characteristic figure for equipment and shows what percentage of the total pollution has been retained in the device.The actual effectiveness of the device operation is equal to or lower than the assumed operating effectiveness. This is because the device operation conditions (e.g. chemical composition of the flowing particulates and gases, grain composition of particulates, flow of gas, temperature, humidity and pressure of gas and other) as well as its availability.Plants of significant nuisance to air quality are the so-called point sources of pollution emission and  include all organizational units specified by the Minister of Environment Protection and Natural Resources on the basis of the amount of fees paid in 1986 for an annual emission of air pollutants according to rates specified in the Regulation of the Council of Ministers of 13 January 1986 on fees for economic use of the environment and introduction of changes (Journal of Laws No. 7 item 40, with later amendments). Thus, a fixed set of reporting units (plants) is maintained annually, which enables to preserve continuity and comparability of research results. The set may be enlarged only in particular cases e.g. newly launched entities or expanded entities of a high threshold pollution emission.

metadata.name: 13.D.2 Share of particulates pollutants retained or neutralised in pollutant reduction systems in percent of pollutants generated
metadata.priority: Increasing the region's resilience to the effects of climate change
metadata.definition: Share of particulate pollutants retained or neutralised in pollution abatement equipment in total generated pollutants.
metadata.methodology: Particulate pollutant emissions – the introduction into the atmosphere emissions into the atmosphere particulate fragmentation macroscopic and colloidal whose concentration exceeds the average content of these substances in the clean air, negatively impacting on human health and the condition and quality of the environment.Particulate pollutants retained or neutralized in purification devices - the amount of particulate pollutants reduced in end-of-pipe devices used to protect the atmosphere.End-of-pipe equipment – equipment that deals with the disposal and reduction of already generated pollutants. They do not affect the production process (production can be carried out without these devices), but reduce or eliminate pollution after their production (e.g. sewage treatment plant, landfill). Expenditure on such projects, in accordance with the methodology recommended by EUROSTAT, is fully allocated to environmental protection.
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 13.D.3 Number of small water retention facilities
metadata.priority: Increasing the region's resilience to the effects of climate change
metadata.definition: Number of small water retention facilities.
metadata.methodology: Small water retention aims at reduction of the outflow of surface waters, with a balance of the natural environment by ensuring the protection and restoration of water resources. It is the ability to collect, detent and drain water in a particular place and time on the surface or in the soil, groundwater, vegetation and forest bad.Facilities of small retention of water are buildings and equipment for keeping most of the water in its surface and by-surface circulation. They are: - artificial water reservoirs with a capacity of 5 million cubic meters, - fish ponds, - self-weirs and water intake, - other weirs or transporting water.

metadata.name: 13.D.4 Capacity of small water retention facilities (dam³)
metadata.priority: Increasing the region's resilience to the effects of climate change
metadata.definition: Volume of small water retention facilities.
metadata.methodology: Small water retention aims at reduction of the outflow of surface waters, with a balance of the natural environment by ensuring the protection and restoration of water resources. It is the ability to collect, detent and drain water in a particular place and time on the surface or in the soil, groundwater, vegetation and forest bad. Facilities of small retention of water are buildings and equipment for keeping most of the water in its surface and by-surface circulation. They are: - artificial water reservoirs with a capacity of 5 million cubic meters , - fish ponds , - self-weirs and water intake, - other weirs or transporting water.

metadata.name: 13.D.5 Investment outlays for the construction of small water retention facilities
metadata.priority: Increasing the region's resilience to the effects of climate change
metadata.definition: Total investment outlays for creation of small water retention facilities in a year.
metadata.methodology: Investment outlays are financial or tangible outlays, the purpose of which is the creation of new fixed assets or the improvement (rebuilding, enlargement, reconstruction, adaptation or modernization) of existing capital asset items, as well as outlays on so-called initial investments.Small water retention aims at reduction of the outflow of surface waters, with a balance of the natural environment by ensuring the protection and restoration of water resources. It is the ability to collect, detent and drain water in a particular place and time on the surface or in the soil, groundwater, vegetation and forest bad.Facilities of small retention of water are buildings and equipment for keeping most of the water in its surface and by-surface circulation. They are: - artificial water reservoirs with a capacity of 5 million cubic meters, - fish ponds, - self-weirs and water intake, - other weirs or transporting water.

metadata.name: 13.D.6 Length of commissioned flood embankments
metadata.priority: Increasing the region's resilience to the effects of climate change
metadata.definition: Total length of flood embankments commissioned in the reporting year.
metadata.methodology: Flood embankment (flood embankment) - an artificial pile-shaped deposit, most often with a trapezoidal cross-section. They are erected along the river at some distance from its bed, which, surrounding floodplains along the river, create a larger bed for the anticipated flood waters, while preventing these waters from spilling over into the neighbouring areas protected in this way.The flood protection shaft must meet the requirements for hydrotechnical structures set out in the Regulation of the Minister of the Environment of 20 April 2007 on the technical conditions to be met by hydrotechnical structures and their location.The construction of flood embankments is classified as an investment in water management.
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development / Chief Inspectorate of Environmental Protection / Ministry of Climate and Environment

metadata.name: 13.D.7 Share of green areas in the total area
metadata.priority: Increasing the region's resilience to the effects of climate change
metadata.definition: Share of green areas in the total area of Poland.
metadata.methodology: Green areas - areas including technical infrastructure and buildings functionally associated with them, covered with vegetation, located in the village of dense buildings or cities, used for the aesthetic, recreational, therapeutic or shielding purposes, in particular parks, lawns, promenades, boulevards, botanical and zoological gardens, children's playgrounds, historic garden, cemeteries, green areas located near roads in the build up areas, squares, historic fortifications, buildings, landfill sites, airports, railway stations and industrial buildings.

metadata.name: 14.A.1 Share of voivodship in national marine fish catches
metadata.priority: Efficient and sustainable use of marine resources for various social and economic purposes, while ensuring the durability of non-renewable resources and natural processes in the perspective of the current and future generations in the region
metadata.definition: Share of voivodship in national marine fish catches.
metadata.methodology: Sea catches – catch data includes all fisheries products landed or transhipped at sea, regardless of their form. It does not include quantities that are thrown overboard, consumed on board, or used as fishing bait. These data refer to catches made by vessels based in one of three voivodeships (West Pomeranian Voivodeship, Pomeranian Voivodeship, Warmian-Masurian Voivodeship).

metadata.name: 14.A.2 Occupancy rate of bed spaces in tourist accommodation establishments in coastal areas
metadata.priority: Efficient and sustainable use of marine resources for various social and economic purposes, while ensuring the durability of non-renewable resources and natural processes in the perspective of the current and future generations in the region
metadata.definition: Share of accommodation occupancy in tourist accommodation facilities in coastal areas.
metadata.methodology: Accommodation surveys are conducted monthly using KT-1 statistical form. This survey provides information on the condition and use of tourist accommodation. Presented information concerns accommodation facilities with 10 or more beds.Number of facilities and accommodations – data includes all facilities open on July 31st, as well as facilities closed on that day but open on other days of the month under review; the maximum capacity was assumed for these facilities. Intervals between stays, e.g., for renovations or disinfection, are not included in the number of days of operation.Number of overnight stays is the product of the number of people (tourists) and the number of days (nights) of their stay.Active establishment – an establishment which was accessible to tourists, at least in part (regardless of its actual occupancy), during the surveyed period.Coastal area – according to European Union tourism statistics, local units (a gmina) that has a sea border or more than 50% of its area is located at the distance of 10 km from the sea:• additionaly, the rural gminas of Słupsk, Główczyce, Pruszcz Gdański, Cedry Wielkie, the urban gmina of Koszalin and the mixed urban-rural gmina of Sianów have been included in agreement with Eurostat as a coastal area,• Szczecin is not included in the EU coastal regions list, but has been added to this publication owing to its location at internal marine waters and its close association with the sea,• Cedry Wielkie (gmina in the Pomorskie Voivodship), Braniewo (gmina in the Warmińsko-Mazurskie Voivodship), and Nowe Warpno (gmina in the Zachodniopomorskie Voivodeship) were not includeddue to the lack of tourist accommodation establishments in the analyzed period.Tourist accommodation establishments – establishments surveyed by Statistics Poland: include: hotels, motels, guesthouses, other hotel facilities (e.g., a facility fulfilling the functions of a hotel, motel, boarding house, (for example an uncategorized establishment functioning as a hotel, motel or boarding house) similar establishment, excursion hostel, shelter, youth hostel, school youth hostel, holiday centre, holiday youth centre, training-recreational centre, creative arts centre, of tourist cottages, camping site, tent camp site, hostel, health resort, rooms for guests, agritourist lodging, other not classified establishments (establishments, which function as tourist accommodation establishments, in the period of not being fully used according to their own purpose, these include among others dormitories, boarding schools, sport and recreational centres, etc.).Tourists staying overnight – individuals (tourists) who began their stay at the facility in a given month, i.e., were checked in. Individuals staying at the turn of two months are counted once (i.e., at the time of check-in).Establishment occupancy rate – results from the comparison of the actual number of provided accommodations with the nominal number of bed places (total bed places prepared for tourists on each activity day of the establishment); it is expressed as a percentage.

metadata.name: 14.A.3 Outflow of organic and biogenous substances by the rivers to the Baltic Sea
metadata.priority: Efficient and sustainable use of marine resources for various social and economic purposes, while ensuring the durability of non-renewable resources and natural processes in the perspective of the current and future generations in the region
metadata.definition: Load of organic and biogenic substances introduced into the Baltic Sea with river runoff.
metadata.methodology: Water analysis is carried out basing on long-term environmental monitoring programs for particular voivodships.River pollutant runoff into the Baltic Sea – information comes from studies conducted as part of the international Baltic Sea monitoring program, stemming from the Helsinki Convention. The Institute of Meteorology and Water Management (IMGW) oversees the research in Poland.Pollution loads discharged into the Baltic Sea by rivers are reported in hydrological years based on monitoring studies of surface flowing waters. The hydrological year covers the period from November 1st to October 31st of the calendar year.Organic substances – carbon-containing chemical compounds that decompose, consuming oxygen, thus polluting the water. They are of natural origin (e.g., proteins, fats, carbohydrates) and synthetic, man-made (e.g., detergents, oils, pesticides, biodegradable plastics).Nutrients – elements and compounds essential for the growth of living organisms, primarily nitrogen (N) and phosphorus (P). Excessive amounts of these nutrients cause eutrophication, i.e., the excessive growth of algae and cyanobacteria, which leads to the degradation of aquatic ecosystems. Nutrients enter the Baltic Sea in two ways:• from internal sources (regeneration of regenerated mineral salts from organic matter, release of phosphates from bottom sediments),• and from external sources (runoff from agricultural land, municipal and domestic sewage, industry, and stormwater).Biochemical oxygen demand (BOD5) is the volume of oxygen consumed within 5 days in biochemical oxidizing process of substances (mainly organic ones) included in waste water, using living bacteria and extracellular enzymes.Chemical oxygen demand (COD) is the amount of oxygen used in chemical oxidation of waste water.Nitrate nitrogen is the contents of nitrogen in the form of nitrate ions. Organic nitrogen is nitrogen bounded in all types of organic compounds, includes amino acids, protein compounds, polypeptides, ureas, and other organic compounds.Amount of nitrogen by Kjeldahl method is a total content of ammonia, nitrate, nitrite and organic nitrogen. Phosphate phosphorus is inorganic orthophosphate, existing in soluble phosphorus.General phosphorus amount is phosphorus consisting soluble and insoluble orthophosphates and condensed phosphates as well as organic compounds and inorganic phosphorus.
metadata.source: Polish Waters National Water Holding / Statistics Poland

metadata.name: 14.A.4 Outflow of heavy metals to the Baltic Sea
metadata.priority: Efficient and sustainable use of marine resources for various social and economic purposes, while ensuring the durability of non-renewable resources and natural processes in the perspective of the current and future generations in the region
metadata.definition: Load of heavy metals introduced into the Baltic Sea with river runoff.
metadata.methodology: Water analysis is carried out basing on long-term environmental monitoring programs for particular voivodships.The charges of pollutants flowed by rivers into the Baltic Sea are presented on the basis of monitoring surveys of flowing surface waters in hydrological years. The hydrological year contains the period from 1 November to 31 October of the calendar year.River pollution runoff into the Baltic Sea – information comes from research conducted as part of the international Baltic Sea monitoring program, stemming from the Helsinki Convention. The Institute of Meteorology and Water Management (IMGW) provides substantive oversight of the research in Poland.Heavy metals are discharged into the Baltic Sea primarily via rivers and atmospheric deposition. They are introduced into the natural environment by human activity (industry and motorization, waste incineration, and the use of chemicals in agriculture), reaching the seas and oceans, where they undergo biochemical transformations and ultimately accumulate in bottom sediments.
metadata.source: Polish Waters National Water Holding / Statistics Poland

metadata.name: 14.A.5 Landings of marine fish and shellfish in Polish ports
metadata.priority: Efficient and sustainable use of marine resources for various social and economic purposes, while ensuring the durability of non-renewable resources and natural processes in the perspective of the current and future generations in the region
metadata.definition: The volume of landings of marine fish and invertebrates in Polish ports.
metadata.methodology: Landings – quantities of fishery products landed on Polish territory by Community and EFTA fishing vessels. Data are collected regardless of the fleet's registration location, pursuant to Regulation (EC) No. 1921/2006.Seaport – an economic facility located at the interface between land and sea, appropriately equipped technically, technologically, and organizationally to handle maritime trade, as well as to handle the maritime and land transport means involved in their transportation.Marine fish – a group encompassing fish species caught in marine waters, including the Baltic Sea and its bays, regardless of their biological origin, including:• freshwater fish – species that spend their entire lives in freshwater, also caught in the Baltic Sea and its bays (the Vistula Lagoon, Szczecin Lagoon); this group includes: gobies, vimba, tusks, ruffs, Siberian sturgeons, crucian carps, carps, silver breams, breams, tenches, burbots, perches, roaches, pikes, pike-perches, catfish, smelts, pikes, and eelpouts,• diadromous fish – species that change habitat between marine and inland waters during their lifespan; This group includes: salmon, rainbow trout, whitefish, sea trout, and eels,• other marine fish – other species of typical marine fish; this group includes, among others: atuni, barracuda, sandeel, dentex, blue ling, motelfish, black scabbardfish, whiting, talismans, tuna, catfish, and yellowtail.Invertebrates – this group includes mollusks (including squid and members of the family Sawfish) and crustaceans (crabs, shrimp).

metadata.name: 14.B.1 Share of the gross value of fixed assets inmaritime economy in total value of gross fixed assets in the voivodeship
metadata.priority: Increasing the share of the maritime economy sector in GDP and increasing employment in the maritime economy
metadata.definition: Share of the gross value of fixed assets used in the maritime economy in total gross value of fixed assets located in the voivodeship.
metadata.methodology: Maritime economy – the maritime economy includes economic activities including the operation of sea ports, maritime navigation, and shipbuilding.A gross value of fixed assets – the value equal to the purchase or manufacture expenses incurred thereon, including the amount of depreciation (amortization).

metadata.name: 14.B.2 Share of people employed in maritime economy in total number of people employed in the voivodeship
metadata.priority: Increasing the share of the maritime economy sector in GDP and increasing employment in the maritime economy
metadata.definition: Share of the number of people employed in activities included in maritime economy in total number of people employed in the voivodeship.
metadata.methodology: Maritime economy – the maritime economy includes economic activities including the operation of sea ports, maritime navigation, and shipbuilding.Employed – people who perform work that brings them earnings (in the form of remuneration for work) or income.

metadata.name: 14.C.1 Share of the voivodship in cargo traffic in seaports
metadata.priority: Strengthening the position of Polish sea ports, increasing the competitiveness of maritime transport and ensuring maritime safety
